Anemia.Grade 3: Hgb <8.0 g/dL; <4.9 mmol/L; <80 g/L; transfusion indicated
Anemia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a reduction in the amount of hemoglobin in 100 ml of blood. Signs and symptoms of anemia may include pallor of the skin and mucous membranes, shortness of breath, palpitations of the heart, soft systolic murmurs, lethargy, and fatigability.

Blood and lymphatic system disorders - O.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Blood and lymphatic system disorders - O.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Blood and lymphatic system disorders - O.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Bone marrow hypocellular.Grade 1: Mildly hypocellular or <=25% reduction from normal cellularity for age
Bone marrow hypocellular.Grade 2: Moderately hypocellular or >25 - <50% reduction from normal cellularity for age
Bone marrow hypocellular.Grade 3: Severely hypocellular or >50 - <=75% reduction cellularity from normal for age
Bone marrow hypocellular.Grade 4: Aplastic persistent for longer than 2 weeks
Bone marrow hypocellular.Definition: A disorder characterized by the inability of the bone marrow to produce hematopoietic elements.

Disseminated intravascular coagulation.Definition: A disorder characterized by systemic pathological activation of blood clotting mechanisms which results in clot formation throughout the body. There is an increase in the risk of hemorrhage as the body is depleted of platelets and coagulation factors.

Eosinophilia.Definition: A disorder characterized by laboratory test results that indicate an increased number of eosinophils in the blood.

Febrile neutropenia.Grade 3: ANC <1000/mm3 with a single temperature of >38.3 degrees C (101 degrees F) or a sustained temperature of >=38 degrees C (100.4 degrees F) for more than one hour
Febrile neutropenia.Definition: A disorder characterized by an ANC <1000/mm3 and a single temperature of >38.3 degrees C (101 degrees F) or a sustained temperature of >=38 degrees C (100.4 degrees F) for more than one hour

Hemolysis.Grade 1: Laboratory evidence of hemolysis only (e.g., direct antiglobulin test; DAT; Coombs'; schistocytes; decreased haptoglobin)
Hemolysis.Grade 2: Evidence of hemolysis and >=2 g decrease in hemoglobin
Hemolysis.Grade 3: Transfusion or medical intervention indicated (e.g., steroids)
Hemolysis.Definition: A disorder characterized by laboratory test results that indicate widespread erythrocyte cell membrane destruction.

Hemolytic uremic syndrome.Grade 3: Laboratory findings with clinical consequences (e.g., renal insufficiency, petechiae)
Hemolytic uremic syndrome.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences, (e.g., CNS hemorrhage or thrombosis/embolism or renal failure)
Hemolytic uremic syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by a form of thrombotic microangiopathy with renal failure, hemolytic anemia, and severe thrombocytopenia.

Leukocytosis.Grade 4: Clinical manifestations of leucostasis; urgent intervention indicated
Leukocytosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by laboratory test results that indicate an increased number of white blood cells in the blood.

Lymph node pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Lymph node pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in a lymph node.

Methemoglobinemia.Definition: A disorder characterized by laboratory test results that indicate increased methemoglobin in the blood.

Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura.Grade 3: Laboratory findings with clinical consequences (e.g., renal insufficiency, petechiae)
Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences, (e.g., CNS hemorrhage or thrombosis/embolism or renal failure)
Thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura.Definition: A disorder characterized by the presence of microangiopathic hemolytic anemia, thrombocytopenic purpura, fever, renal abnormalities and neurological abnormalities such as seizures, hemiplegia, and visual disturbances. It is an acute or subacute condition.

Aortic valve disease.Grade 1: Asymptomatic valvular thickening with or without mild valvular regurgitation or stenosis by imaging
Aortic valve disease.Grade 2: Asymptomatic; moderate regurgitation or stenosis by imaging
Aortic valve disease.Grade 3: Symptomatic; severe regurgitation or stenosis by imaging; symptoms controlled with medical intervention
Aortic valve disease.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., valve replacement, valvuloplasty)
Aortic valve disease.Definition: A disorder characterized by a defect in aortic valve function or structure.

Asystole.Grade 1: Periods of asystole; non-urgent medical management indicated
Asystole.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia without cardiac electrical activity. Typically, this is accompanied by cessation of the pumping function of the heart.

Atrial fibrillation.Grade 3: Symptomatic, urgent intervention indicated; device (e.g., pacemaker); ablation; new onset
Atrial fibrillation.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; embolus requiring urgent intervention
Atrial fibrillation.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia without discernible P waves and an irregular ventricular response due to multiple reentry circuits. The rhythm disturbance originates above the ventricles.

Atrial flutter.Grade 3: Symptomatic, urgent intervention indicated; device (e.g., pacemaker); ablation
Atrial flutter.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; embolus requiring urgent intervention
Atrial flutter.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia with organized rhythmic atrial contractions with a rate of 200-300 beats per minute. The rhythm disturbance originates in the atria.

Atrioventricular block complete.Grade 3: Symptomatic and incompletely controlled medically, or controlled with device (e.g., pacemaker); new onset
Atrioventricular block complete.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia with complete failure of atrial electrical impulse conduction through the AV node to the ventricles.

Atrioventricular block first degree.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia with a delay in the time required for the conduction of an electrical impulse through the atrioventricular (AV) node beyond 0.2 seconds; prolongation of the PR interval greater than 200 milliseconds.

Cardiac arrest.Definition: A disorder characterized by cessation of the pumping function of the heart.

Cardiac disorders - Other, specify.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Cardiac disorders - Other, specify.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Cardiac disorders - Other, specify.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Chest pain - cardiac.Grade 2: Moderate pain; pain on exertion; limiting instrumental ADL; hemodynamically stable
Chest pain - cardiac.Grade 3: Pain at rest; limiting self care ADL; cardiac catheterization; new onset cardiac chest pain; unstable angina
Chest pain - cardiac.Definition: A disorder characterized by substernal discomfort due to insufficient myocardial oxygenation e.g., angina pectoris.
Chest pain - cardiac.Navigational Note: Also consider Cardiac disorders: Myocardial infarction.
Chest pain - cardiac.CTCAE v5.0 Change: Addition: Navigational note; Clarification: Grade 2, 3, Definition

Conduction disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by pathological irregularities in the cardiac conduction system.

Cyanosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a bluish discoloration of the skin and/or mucous membranes.

Heart failure.Grade 1: Asymptomatic with laboratory (e.g., BNP [B-Natriuretic Peptide ]) or cardiac imaging abnormalities
Heart failure.Grade 2: Symptoms with moderate activity or exertion
Heart failure.Grade 3: Symptoms at rest or with minimal activity or exertion; hospitalization; new onset of symptoms
Heart failure.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., continuous IV therapy or mechanical hemodynamic support)
Heart failure.Definition: A disorder characterized by the inability of the heart to pump blood at an adequate volume to meet tissue metabolic requirements, or, the ability to do so only at an elevation in the filling pressure.
Heart failure.Navigational Note: If left sided use Cardiac disorders: Left ventricular systolic dysfunction; also consider Cardiac disorders: Restrictive cardiomyopathy, Investigations: Ejection fraction decreased.

Left ventricular systolic dysfunction.Grade 3: Symptomatic due to drop in ejection fraction responsive to intervention
Left ventricular systolic dysfunction.Grade 4: Refractory or poorly controlled heart failure due to drop in ejection fraction; intervention such as ventricular assist device, intravenous vasopressor support, or heart transplant indicated
Left ventricular systolic dysfunction.Definition: A disorder characterized by failure of the left ventricle to produce adequate output.
Left ventricular systolic dysfunction.Navigational Note: Also consider Investigations: Ejection fraction decreased.

Mitral valve disease.Grade 1: Asymptomatic valvular thickening with or without mild valvular regurgitation or stenosis by imaging
Mitral valve disease.Grade 2: Asymptomatic; moderate regurgitation or stenosis by imaging
Mitral valve disease.Grade 3: Symptomatic; severe regurgitation or stenosis by imaging; symptoms controlled with medical intervention
Mitral valve disease.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., valve replacement, valvuloplasty)
Mitral valve disease.Definition: A disorder characterized by a defect in mitral valve function or structure.

Mobitz (type) II atrioventricular block.Grade 3: Symptomatic and incompletely controlled medically, or controlled with device (e.g., pacemaker); new onset
Mobitz (type) II atrioventricular block.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia with relatively constant PR interval prior to the block of an atrial impulse. This is the result of intermittent failure of atrial electrical impulse conduction through the atrioventricular (AV) node to the ventricles.

Mobitz type I.Grade 3: Symptomatic and incompletely controlled medically, or controlled with device (e.g., pacemaker)
Mobitz type I.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia with a progressively lengthening PR interval prior to the blocking of an atrial impulse. This is the result of intermittent failure of atrial electrical impulse conduction through the atrioventricular (AV) node to the ventricles.

Myocardial infarction.Grade 2: Asymptomatic and cardiac enzymes minimally abnormal and no evidence of ischemic ECG changes
Myocardial infarction.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; cardiac enzymes abnormal; hemodynamically stable; ECG changes consistent with infarction
Myocardial infarction.Definition: A disorder characterized by gross necrosis of the myocardium; this is due to an interruption of blood supply to the area.

Myocarditis.Grade 2: Symptoms with moderate activity or exertion
Myocarditis.Grade 3: Severe with symptoms at rest or with minimal activity or exertion; intervention indicated; new onset of symptoms
Myocarditis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., continuous IV therapy or mechanical hemodynamic support)
Myocarditis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the muscle tissue of the heart.

Palpitations.Definition: A disorder characterized by an unpleasant sensation of irregular and/or forceful beating of the heart.

Paroxysmal atrial tachycardia.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; incompletely controlled medically; cardioversion indicated
Paroxysmal atrial tachycardia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia with abrupt onset and sudden termination of atrial contractions with a rate of 150-250 beats per minute. The rhythm disturbance originates in the atria.

Pericardial effusion.Grade 2: Asymptomatic effusion size small to moderate
Pericardial effusion.Definition: A disorder characterized by fluid collection within the pericardial sac, usually due to inflammation.

Pericardial tamponade.Definition: A disorder characterized by an increase in intrapericardial pressure due to the collection of blood or fluid in the pericardium.

Pericarditis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic, ECG or physical findings (e.g., rub) consistent with pericarditis
Pericarditis.Grade 3: Pericarditis with physiologic consequences (e.g., pericardial constriction)
Pericarditis.Definition: A disorder characterized by irritation to the layers of the pericardium (the protective sac around the heart).

Pulmonary valve disease.Grade 1: Asymptomatic valvular thickening with or without mild valvular regurgitation or stenosis by imaging
Pulmonary valve disease.Grade 2: Asymptomatic; moderate regurgitation or stenosis by imaging
Pulmonary valve disease.Grade 3: Symptomatic; severe regurgitation or stenosis by imaging; symptoms controlled with medical intervention
Pulmonary valve disease.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., valve replacement, valvuloplasty)
Pulmonary valve disease.Definition: A disorder characterized by a defect in pulmonary valve function or structure.

Restrictive cardiomyopathy.Grade 2: Symptomatic without signs of heart failure
Restrictive cardiomyopathy.Grade 3: Symptomatic heart failure or other cardiac symptoms, responsive to intervention; new onset of symptoms
Restrictive cardiomyopathy.Grade 4: Refractory heart failure or other poorly controlled cardiac symptoms
Restrictive cardiomyopathy.Definition: A disorder characterized by an inability of the ventricles to fill with blood because the myocardium (heart muscle) stiffens and loses its flexibility.

Right ventricular dysfunction.Grade 1: Asymptomatic with laboratory (e.g., BNP [B-Natriuretic Peptide ]) or cardiac imaging abnormalities
Right ventricular dysfunction.Grade 2: Symptoms with moderate activity or exertion
Right ventricular dysfunction.Grade 3: Severe symptoms, associated with hypoxia, right heart failure; oxygen indicated
Right ventricular dysfunction.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., ventricular assist device); heart transplant indicated
Right ventricular dysfunction.Definition: A disorder characterized by impairment of right ventricular function associated with low ejection fraction and a decrease in motility of the right ventricular wall.

Sick sinus syndrome.Grade 2: Symptomatic, intervention not indicated; change in medication initiated
Sick sinus syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia with alternating periods of bradycardia and atrial tachycardia accompanied by syncope, fatigue and dizziness.

Sinus bradycardia.Grade 2: Symptomatic, intervention not indicated; change in medication initiated
Sinus bradycardia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia with a heart rate less than 60 beats per minute that originates in the sinus node.

Sinus tachycardia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia with a heart rate greater than 100 beats per minute that originates in the sinus node.

Supraventricular tachycardia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia with a heart rate greater than 100 beats per minute that originates above the ventricles.

Tricuspid valve disease.Grade 1: Asymptomatic valvular thickening with or without mild valvular regurgitation or stenosis
Tricuspid valve disease.Grade 2: Asymptomatic; moderate regurgitation or stenosis by imaging
Tricuspid valve disease.Grade 3: Symptomatic; severe regurgitation or stenosis; symptoms controlled with medical intervention
Tricuspid valve disease.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., valve replacement, valvuloplasty)
Tricuspid valve disease.Definition: A disorder characterized by a defect in tricuspid valve function or structure.

Ventricular arrhythmia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia that originates in the ventricles.

Ventricular fibrillation.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia without discernible QRS complexes due to rapid repetitive excitation of myocardial fibers without coordinated contraction of the ventricles.

Ventricular tachycardia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a dysrhythmia with a heart rate greater than 100 beats per minute that originates distal to the bundle of His.

Congenital, familial and genetic disorde.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Congenital, familial and genetic disorde.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Congenital, familial and genetic disorde.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Ear and labyrinth disorders - Other, spe.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Ear and labyrinth disorders - Other, spe.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Ear and labyrinth disorders - Other, spe.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Ear pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Ear pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the ear.

External ear pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
External ear pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the external ear region.

Hearing impaired.Grade 4:
Adults: Decrease in hearing to profound bilateral loss (absolute threshold >80 dB HL at 2 kHz and above); nonservicable hearing

Pediatric: Audiologic indication for cochlear implant; > 40 dB HL (i.e., 45 dB HL or more); SNHL at 2 kHz and above
Hearing impaired.Definition: A disorder characterized by partial or complete loss of the ability to detect or understand sounds resulting from damage to ear structures.

Middle ear inflammation.Grade 3: Mastoiditis; necrosis of canal soft tissue or bone
Middle ear inflammation.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation (physiologic response to irritation), swelling and redness to the middle ear.

Tinnitus.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Tinnitus.Definition: A disorder characterized by noise in the ears, such as ringing, buzzing, roaring or clicking.

Vertigo.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Vertigo.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation as if the external world were revolving around the patient (objective vertigo) or as if he himself were revolving in space (subjective vertigo).

Vestibular disorder.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Vestibular disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by dizziness, imbalance, nausea, and vision problems.

Adrenal insufficiency.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Adrenal insufficiency.Definition: A disorder characterized by the adrenal cortex not producing enough of the hormone cortisol and in some cases, the hormone aldosterone. It may be due to a disorder of the adrenal cortex as in Addison's disease or primary adrenal insufficiency.

Cushingoid.Grade 3: Severe symptoms, medical intervention or hospitalization indicated
Cushingoid.Definition: A disorder characterized by signs and symptoms that resemble Cushing's disease or syndrome: buffalo hump obesity, striations, adiposity, hypertension, diabetes, and osteoporosis, usually due to exogenous corticosteroids.

Delayed puberty.Grade 2: No breast development by age 13 yrs for females; testes volume of <3 cc or no Tanner Stage 2 development by age 14.5 yrs for males
Delayed puberty.Grade 3: No breast development by age 14 yrs for females; no increase in testes volume or no Tanner Stage 2 by age 16 yrs for males; hormone replacement indicated
Delayed puberty.Definition: A disorder characterized by unusually late sexual maturity.

Endocrine disorders - Other, specify.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Endocrine disorders - Other, specify.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Endocrine disorders - Other, specify.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Growth accelerated.Grade 2: >= +2 SD (standard deviation) above mid parental height or target height
Growth accelerated.Definition: A disorder characterized by greater growth than expected for age.

Hyperparathyroidism.Definition: A disorder characterized by an increase in production of parathyroid hormone by the parathyroid glands. This results in hypercalcemia (abnormally high levels of calcium in the blood).

Hyperthyroidism.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hyperthyroidism.Grade 2: Symptomatic; thyroid suppression therapy indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Hyperthyroidism.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL; hospitalization indicated
Hyperthyroidism.Definition: A disorder characterized by excessive levels of thyroid hormone in the body. Common causes include an overactive thyroid gland or thyroid hormone overdose.

Hypoparathyroidism.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hypoparathyroidism.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; medical intervention or hospitalization indicated
Hypoparathyroidism.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in production of parathyroid hormone by the parathyroid glands.

Hypophysitis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hypophysitis.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Hypophysitis.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL
Hypophysitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation and cellular infiltration of the pituitary gland.

Hypopituitarism.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hypopituitarism.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Hypopituitarism.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL
Hypopituitarism.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in production of hormones from the pituitary gland.

Hypothyroidism.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hypothyroidism.Grade 2: Symptomatic; thyroid replacement indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Hypothyroidism.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL; hospitalization indicated
Hypothyroidism.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in production of thyroid hormone by the thyroid gland.

Precocious puberty.Grade 1: Physical signs of puberty with no biochemical markers for females <8 years and males <9 years
Precocious puberty.Grade 2: Physical signs and biochemical markers of puberty for females <8 years and males <9 years
Precocious puberty.Definition: A disorder characterized by unusually early development of secondary sexual features; the onset of sexual maturation begins usually before age 8 for girls and before age 9 for boys.

Testosterone deficiency.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; mild symptoms with no intervention indicated
Testosterone deficiency.Definition: A disorder characterized by low testosterone.

Virilization.Definition: A disorder characterized by inappropriate masculinization occurring in a female or prepubertal male.

Blurred vision.Grade 2: Symptomatic; moderate decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity 20/40 and better or 3 lines or less decreased vision from known baseline); limiting instrumental ADL
Blurred vision.Grade 3: Symptomatic with marked decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity worse than 20/40 or more than 3 lines of decreased vision from known baseline, up to 20/200); limiting self care ADL
Blurred vision.Grade 4: Best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye
Blurred vision.Definition: A disorder characterized by visual perception of unclear or fuzzy images.

Cataract.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Cataract.Grade 2: Symptomatic; moderate decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity 20/40 and better or 3 lines or less decreased vision from known baseline); glare symptoms affecting instrumental ADL
Cataract.Grade 3: Symptomatic with marked decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity worse than 20/40 or more than 3 lines of decreased vision from known baseline, up to 20/200); limiting self care ADL
Cataract.Grade 4: Best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye
Cataract.Definition: A disorder characterized by partial or complete opacity of the crystalline lens of one or both eyes. This results in a decrease in visual acuity and eventual blindness if untreated.

Corneal ulcer.Grade 3: Corneal ulcer without perforation in the affected eye
Corneal ulcer.Definition: A disorder characterized by an area of epithelial tissue loss on the surface of the cornea. It is associated with inflammatory cells in the cornea and anterior chamber.

Dry eye.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; symptoms relieved by lubricants
Dry eye.Grade 2: Symptomatic; moderate decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity 20/40 and better or 3 lines or less decreased vision from known baseline)
Dry eye.Grade 3: Symptomatic with marked decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity worse than 20/40 or more than 3 lines of decreased vision from known baseline, up to 20/200); limiting self care ADL
Dry eye.Definition: A disorder characterized by dryness of the cornea and conjunctiva.
Dry eye.Navigational Note: If corneal ulcer is present, grade under Eye disorders: Corneal ulcer.

Extraocular muscle paresis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only
Extraocular muscle paresis.Grade 3: Bilateral paresis or unilateral paresis causing double vision in peripheral gaze, but not in central gaze
Extraocular muscle paresis.Grade 4: Bilateral paresis requiring head turning to see beyond central 60 degrees or double vision in central gaze
Extraocular muscle paresis.Definition: A disorder characterized by incomplete paralysis of an extraocular muscle.

Eye disorders - Other, specify.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated; no change in vision
Eye disorders - Other, specify.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL; best corrected visual acuity 20/40 and better or 3 lines or less decreased vision from known baseline
Eye disorders - Other, specify.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately sight-threatening; limiting self care ADL; decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity worse than 20/40 or more than 3 lines of decreased vision from known baseline, up to 20/200)
Eye disorders - Other, specify.Grade 4: Sight-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated; best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye

Eye pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Eye pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the eye.

Eyelid function disorder.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Eyelid function disorder.Grade 2: Symptomatic; nonoperative intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Eyelid function disorder.Grade 3: Limiting self care ADL; operative intervention indicated
Eyelid function disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by impaired eyelid function.

Flashing lights.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sudden or brief burst of light.
Flashing lights.Navigational Note: Also consider Eye disorders: Retinal tear or Retinal detachment

Floaters.Definition: A disorder characterized by an individual seeing spots before their eyes. The spots are shadows of opaque cell fragments in the vitreous humor or lens.
Floaters.Navigational Note: Also consider Eye disorders: Retinal tear or Retinal detachment

Glaucoma.Grade 1: Less than 8 mmHg of elevated intraocular pressure (EIOP); no visual field deficit
Glaucoma.Grade 2: EIOP which can be reduced to 21 mmHg or under with topical medications and no visual field deficit
Glaucoma.Grade 4: Visual field deficit within the central 10 degrees of the visual field in the affected eye
Glaucoma.Definition: A disorder characterized by an increase in pressure in the eyeball due to obstruction of the aqueous humor outflow.

Keratitis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Keratitis.Grade 2: Symptomatic; moderate decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity 20/40 and better or 3 lines or less decreased vision from known baseline)
Keratitis.Grade 3: Symptomatic with marked decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity worse than 20/40 or more than 3 lines of decreased vision from known baseline, up to 20/200); corneal ulcer; limiting self care ADL
Keratitis.Grade 4: Perforation; best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye
Keratitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation to the cornea of the eye.
Keratitis.Navigational Note: Also consider Eye disorders: Corneal ulcer
Keratitis.CTCAE v5.0 Change: Addition: Grade 1, Navigational note; Clarification: Grade 2, 3, 4

Night blindness.Grade 2: Symptomatic; moderate decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity 20/40 and better or 3 lines or less decreased vision from known baseline); limiting instrumental ADL
Night blindness.Grade 3: Symptomatic with marked decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity worse than 20/40 or more than 3 lines of decreased vision from known baseline, up to 20/200); limiting self care ADL
Night blindness.Grade 4: Best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye
Night blindness.Definition: A disorder characterized by an inability to see clearly in dim light.

Optic nerve disorder.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only
Optic nerve disorder.Grade 2: Moderate decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity 20/40 and better or 3 lines or less decreased vision from known baseline)
Optic nerve disorder.Grade 3: Marked decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity worse than 20/40 or more than 3 lines of decreased vision from known baseline, up to 20/200)
Optic nerve disorder.Grade 4: Best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye
Optic nerve disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by involvement of the optic nerve (second cranial nerve).

Papilledema.Grade 2: Symptomatic; moderate decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity 20/40 and better or 3 lines or less decreased vision from known baseline)
Papilledema.Grade 3: Symptomatic with marked decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity worse than 20/40 or more than 3 lines of decreased vision from known baseline, up to 20/200)
Papilledema.Grade 4: Best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye
Papilledema.Definition: A disorder characterized by swelling around the optic disc.

Periorbital edema.Grade 2: Indurated or pitting edema; topical intervention indicated
Periorbital edema.Grade 3: Edema associated with visual disturbance; increased intraocular pressure, glaucoma or retinal hemorrhage; optic neuritis; diuretics indicated; operative intervention indicated
Periorbital edema.Definition: A disorder characterized by swelling due to an excessive accumulation of fluid around the orbits of the face.

Photophobia.Definition: A disorder characterized by fear and avoidance of light.

Retinal detachment.Definition: A disorder characterized by the separation of the inner retina layers from the underlying pigment epithelium.

Retinal tear.Grade 1: No retinal detachment and treatment not indicated
Retinal tear.Grade 2: No retinal detachment and treatment indicated
Retinal tear.Definition: A disorder characterized by a small laceration of the retina, this occurs when the vitreous separates from the retina. Symptoms include flashes and floaters.
Retinal tear.Navigational Note: If retinal detachment is present, grade under Eye disorders: Retinal detachment
Retinal tear.CTCAE v5.0 Change: Addition: Grade 1, Navigational note; Clarification: Grade 2; Deletion: Grade 3, 4

Retinal vascular disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by pathological retinal blood vessels that adversely affects vision.
Retinal vascular disorder.Navigational Note: If vitreous hemorrhage is present, report under Eye disorders: Vitreous hemorrhage.

Retinopathy.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only
Retinopathy.Grade 2: Symptomatic; moderate decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity 20/40 and better or 3 lines or less decreased vision from known baseline); limiting instrumental ADL
Retinopathy.Grade 3: Symptomatic with marked decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity worse than 20/40 or more than 3 lines of decreased vision from known baseline, up to 20/200); limiting self care ADL
Retinopathy.Grade 4: Best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye
Retinopathy.Navigational Note: If vitreous hemorrhage is present, report under Eye disorders: Vitreous hemorrhage.

Scleral disorder.Grade 1: No change in vision from baseline
Scleral disorder.Grade 2: Symptomatic; moderate decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity 20/40 and better or 3 lines or less decreased vision from known baseline); limiting instrumental ADL
Scleral disorder.Grade 3: Symptomatic with marked decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity worse than 20/40 or more than 3 lines of decreased vision from known baseline, up to 20/200); limiting self care ADL
Scleral disorder.Grade 4: Best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye
Scleral disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by involvement of the sclera of the eye.

Uveitis.Grade 3: Anterior uveitis with 3+ or greater cells; intermediate posterior or pan-uveitis
Uveitis.Grade 4: Best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye
Uveitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation to the uvea of the eye.

Vision decreased.Grade 2: Moderate decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity 20/40 and better or 3 lines or less decreased vision from known baseline)
Vision decreased.Grade 3: Marked decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity worse than 20/40 or more than 3 lines of decreased vision from known baseline, up to 20/200)
Vision decreased.Grade 4: Best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye
Vision decreased.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in visual acuity.
Vision decreased.Navigational Note: If etiology is known, use a more specific CTCAE term.

Vitreous hemorrhage.Grade 2: Symptomatic; moderate decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity 20/40 and better or 3 lines or less decreased vision from known baseline); limiting instrumental ADL
Vitreous hemorrhage.Grade 3: Symptomatic with marked decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity worse than 20/40 or more than 3 lines of decreased vision from known baseline, up to 20/200); limiting self care ADL; vitrectomy indicated
Vitreous hemorrhage.Grade 4: Best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye
Vitreous hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding into the vitreous humor.

Vomiting.Grade 2: Outpatient IV hydration; medical intervention indicated
Vomiting.Grade 3: Tube feeding, TPN, or hospitalization indicated
Vomiting.Definition: A disorder characterized by the reflexive act of ejecting the contents of the stomach through the mouth.

Chills.Grade 1: Mild sensation of cold; shivering; chattering of teeth
Chills.Grade 2: Moderate tremor of the entire body; narcotics indicated
Chills.Grade 3: Severe or prolonged, not responsive to narcotics
Chills.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of cold that often marks a physiologic response to sweating after a fever.

Death neonatal.Definition: Newborn death occurring during the first 28 days after birth.
Death neonatal.CTCAE v5.0 Change: Addition: Grade 4; Clarification: Definition; Deletion: Grade 5

Death NOS.Definition: Death that cannot be attributed to a CTCAE term associated with Grade 5.
Death NOS.Navigational Note: If death is due to an AE (ex., Cardiac disorders: Cardiac arrest), report as a Grade 5 event under that AE.

Disease progression.Definition: Death due to disease progression that cannot be attributed to a CTCAE term associated with Grade 5.
Disease progression.Navigational Note: If death is due to an AE (ex., Cardiac disorders: Cardiac arrest), report as a Grade 5 event under that AE.

Edema face.Grade 2: Moderate localized facial edema; limiting instrumental ADL
Edema face.Grade 3: Severe swelling; limiting self care ADL
Edema face.Definition: A disorder characterized by swelling due to excessive fluid accumulation in facial tissues.

Edema limbs.Grade 1: 5 - 10% inter-limb discrepancy in volume or circumference at point of greatest visible difference; swelling or obscuration of anatomic architecture on close inspection
Edema limbs.Grade 2: >10 - 30% inter-limb discrepancy in volume or circumference at point of greatest visible difference; readily apparent obscuration of anatomic architecture; obliteration of skin folds; readily apparent deviation from normal anatomic contour; limiting instrumental ADL
Edema limbs.Grade 3: >30% inter-limb discrepancy in volume; gross deviation from normal anatomic contour; limiting self care ADL
Edema limbs.Definition: A disorder characterized by swelling due to excessive fluid accumulation in the upper or lower extremities.

Edema trunk.Grade 1: Swelling or obscuration of anatomic architecture on close inspection
Edema trunk.Grade 2: Readily apparent obscuration of anatomic architecture; obliteration of skin folds; readily apparent deviation from normal anatomic contour; limiting instrumental ADL
Edema trunk.Grade 3: Gross deviation from normal anatomic contour; limiting self care ADL
Edema trunk.Definition: A disorder characterized by swelling due to excessive fluid accumulation in the trunk area.

Facial pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Facial pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the face.

Fatigue.Grade 2: Fatigue not relieved by rest; limiting instrumental ADL
Fatigue.Grade 3: Fatigue not relieved by rest, limiting self care ADL
Fatigue.Definition: A disorder characterized by a state of generalized weakness with a pronounced inability to summon sufficient energy to accomplish daily activities.

Fever.Grade 3: >40.0 degrees C (>104.0 degrees F) for <=24 hrs
Fever.Grade 4: >40.0 degrees C (>104.0 degrees F) for >24 hrs
Fever.Definition: A disorder characterized by elevation of the body's temperature above the upper limit of normal.

Flu like symptoms.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Flu like symptoms.Definition: A disorder characterized by a group of symptoms similar to those observed in patients with the flu. It includes fever, chills, body aches, malaise, loss of appetite and dry cough.

Gait disturbance.Grade 1: Mild change in gait (e.g., wide-based, limping or hobbling)
Gait disturbance.Grade 2: Moderate change in gait (e.g., wide-based, limping or hobbling); assistive device indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Gait disturbance.Definition: A disorder characterized by walking difficulties.

General disorders and administration sit.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
General disorders and administration sit.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
General disorders and administration sit.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Generalized edema.Grade 2: Interfering with instrumental ADLs; oral therapy initiated
Generalized edema.Grade 3: Interferes with self care ADL; intravenous therapy indicated; skin breakdown
Generalized edema.Definition: A disorder characterized by fluid accumulation in the tissues of the body including the skin.

Hypothermia.Grade 4: <=28 degrees C; 82.4 degrees F; life-threatening consequences (e.g., coma, hypotension, pulmonary edema, acidemia, ventricular fibrillation)
Hypothermia.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormally low body temperature. Treatment is required when the body temperature is 35C (95F) or below.

Infusion site extravasation.Grade 2: Erythema with associated symptoms (e.g., edema, pain, induration, phlebitis)
Infusion site extravasation.Grade 3: Ulceration or necrosis; severe tissue damage; operative intervention indicated
Infusion site extravasation.Definition: A disorder characterized by leakage of the infusion into the surrounding tissue. Signs and symptoms may include induration, erythema, swelling, burning sensation and marked discomfort at the infusion site.

Injection site reaction.Grade 1: Tenderness with or without associated symptoms (e.g., warmth, erythema, itching)
Injection site reaction.Grade 3: Ulceration or necrosis; severe tissue damage; operative intervention indicated
Injection site reaction.Definition: A disorder characterized by an intense adverse reaction (usually immunologic) developing at the site of an injection.

Localized edema.Grade 1: Localized to dependent areas, no disability or functional impairment
Localized edema.Grade 2: Moderate localized edema and intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Localized edema.Grade 3: Severe localized edema and intervention indicated; limiting self care ADL
Localized edema.Definition: A disorder characterized by swelling due to excessive fluid accumulation at a specific anatomic site.
Localized edema.Navigational Note: Prior to using this term consider specific edema areas: General disorders and administration site conditions: Edema face, Edema limbs, Edema trunk, or Edema neck; Nervous system disorders: Edema cerebral; Reproductive system and breast disorders: Genital edema; Respiratory, thoracic and mediastinal disorders: Laryngeal edema or Pulmonary edema; Skin and subcutaneous tissue disorders: Periorbital edema; Vascular disorders: Lymphedema

Malaise.Grade 1: Uneasiness or lack of well being
Malaise.Grade 2: Uneasiness or lack of well being limiting instrumental ADL
Malaise.Grade 3: Uneasiness or lack of well being limiting self-care ADL
Malaise.Definition: A disorder characterized by a feeling of general discomfort or uneasiness, an out-of-sorts feeling.

Multi-organ failure.Grade 3: Shock with azotemia and acid-base disturbances; significant coagulation abnormalities
Multi-organ failure.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences (e.g., vasopressor dependent and oliguric or anuric or ischemic colitis or lactic acidosis)
Multi-organ failure.Definition: A disorder characterized by progressive deterioration of the lungs, liver, kidney and clotting mechanisms.

Neck edema.Grade 2: Moderate neck edema; slight obliteration of anatomic landmarks; limiting instrumental ADL
Neck edema.Grade 3: Generalized neck edema (e.g., difficulty in turning neck); limiting self care ADL
Neck edema.Grade 4: Vascular or respiratory impairment requiring urgent intervention
Neck edema.Definition: A disorder characterized by swelling due to an accumulation of excessive fluid in the neck.

Non-cardiac chest pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Non-cardiac chest pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the chest unrelated to a heart disorder.

Pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by the sensation of marked discomfort, distress or agony.
Pain.Navigational Note: Prior to using this term consider using a specific body part pain term found throughout the CTCAE (over 40 different pain terms).

Sudden death NOS.Definition: An unexpected death that cannot be attributed to a CTCAE term associated with Grade 5.
Sudden death NOS.Navigational Note: If death is due to an AE (ex., Cardiac disorders: Cardiac arrest), report as a Grade 5 event under that AE.

Vaccination site lymphadenopathy.Grade 2: Localized ulceration; generalized lymph node enlargement
Vaccination site lymphadenopathy.Definition: A disorder characterized by lymph node enlargement after vaccination

Bile duct stenosis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Bile duct stenosis.Grade 2: Symptomatic; altered GI function; IV fluids indicated <24 hrs
Bile duct stenosis.Grade 3: Severely altered GI function; invasive intervention indicated
Bile duct stenosis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent operative intervention indicated
Bile duct stenosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a narrowing of the lumen of the bile duct.

Biliary fistula.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal communication between the bile ducts and another organ or anatomic site.

Budd-Chiari syndrome.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; asterixis; mild encephalopathy
Budd-Chiari syndrome.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; moderate to severe encephalopathy; coma
Budd-Chiari syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by occlusion of the hepatic veins and typically presents with abdominal pain, ascites and hepatomegaly.

Cholecystitis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent operative intervention indicated
Cholecystitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation involving the gallbladder. It may be associated with the presence of gallstones.

Gallbladder fistula.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal communication between the gallbladder and another organ or anatomic site.

Gallbladder necrosis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent invasive intervention indicated
Gallbladder necrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a necrotic process occurring in the gallbladder.

Gallbladder obstruction.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Gallbladder obstruction.Grade 2: Symptomatic; altered GI function; IV fluids indicated <24 hrs
Gallbladder obstruction.Grade 3: Symptomatic and severely altered GI function; tube feeding, TPN or hospitalization indicated; non-emergent operative intervention indicated
Gallbladder obstruction.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent operative intervention indicated
Gallbladder obstruction.Definition: A disorder characterized by blockage of the normal flow of the contents of the gallbladder.

Gallbladder pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Gallbladder pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the gallbladder region.

Gallbladder perforation.Definition: A disorder characterized by a rupture in the gallbladder wall.

Hepatic failure.Grade 3: Asterixis; mild encephalopathy; drug-induced liver injury (DILI); limiting self care ADL
Hepatic failure.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; moderate to severe encephalopathy; coma
Hepatic failure.Definition: A disorder characterized by the inability of the liver to metabolize chemicals in the body. Laboratory test results reveal abnormal plasma levels of ammonia, bilirubin, lactic dehydrogenase, alkaline phosphatase, aminotransferase, and/or prolongation of prothrombin time (INR.) Drug-induced liever injury (DILI) as defined by Hy's Law.

Hepatic hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Hepatic hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the liver.

Hepatic necrosis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent invasive intervention indicated
Hepatic necrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a necrotic process occurring in the hepatic parenchyma.

Hepatic pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Hepatic pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the liver region.

Hepatobiliary disorders - Other, specify.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hepatobiliary disorders - Other, specify.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Hepatobiliary disorders - Other, specify.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Perforation bile duct.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent operative intervention indicated
Perforation bile duct.Definition: A disorder characterized by a rupture in the wall of the extrahepatic or intrahepatic bile duct.

Portal hypertension.Grade 3: Reversal/retrograde portal vein flow; associated with varices and/or ascites
Portal hypertension.Definition: A disorder characterized by an increase in blood pressure in the portal venous system.

Portal vein thrombosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by the formation of a thrombus (blood clot) in the portal vein.

Sinusoidal obstruction syndrome.Grade 2: Blood bilirubin 2-5 mg/dL; minor interventions required (i.e., blood product, diuretic, oxygen)
Sinusoidal obstruction syndrome.Grade 3: Blood bilirubin >5 mg/dL; coagulation modifier indicated (e.g., defibrotide); reversal of flow on ultrasound
Sinusoidal obstruction syndrome.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences (e.g., ventilatory support, dialysis, plasmapheresis, peritoneal drainage)
Sinusoidal obstruction syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by severe hepatic injury as a result of the blood vessels of the liver becoming inflamed and/or blocked.

Allergic reaction.Grade 3: Bronchospasm; hospitalization indicated for clinical sequelae; intravenous intervention indicated
Allergic reaction.Definition: A disorder characterized by an adverse local or general response from exposure to an allergen.
Allergic reaction.Navigational Note: If related to infusion, use Injury, poisoning and procedural complications: Infusion related reaction. Do not report both.

Anaphylaxis.Grade 3: Symptomatic bronchospasm, with or without urticaria; parenteral intervention indicated; allergy-related edema/angioedema; hypotension
Anaphylaxis.Definition: A disorder characterized by an acute inflammatory reaction resulting from the release of histamine and histamine-like substances from mast cells, causing a hypersensitivity immune response. Clinically, it presents with breathing difficulty, dizziness, hypotension, cyanosis and loss of consciousness and may lead to death.

Autoimmune disorder.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; serologic or other evidence of autoimmune reaction, with normal organ function; intervention not indicated
Autoimmune disorder.Grade 2: Evidence of autoimmune reaction involving a non-essential organ or function (e.g., hypothyroidism)
Autoimmune disorder.Grade 3: Autoimmune reactions involving major organ (e.g., colitis, anemia, myocarditis, kidney)
Autoimmune disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by loss of function or tissue destruction of an organ or multiple organs, arising from humoral or cellular immune responses of the individual to his own tissue constituents.
Autoimmune disorder.Navigational Note: Prior to using this term consider specific autoimmune AEs

Cytokine release syndrome.Grade 1: Fever with or without constitutional symptoms
Cytokine release syndrome.Grade 2: Hypotension responding to fluids; hypoxia responding to <40% O2
Cytokine release syndrome.Grade 3: Hypotension managed with one pressor; hypoxia requiring ≥ 40% O2
Cytokine release syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by fever, tachypnea, headache, tachycardia, hypotension, rash, and/or hypoxia caused by the release of cytokines.
Cytokine release syndrome.Navigational Note: Also consider reporting other organ dysfunctions including neurological toxicities such as: Psychiatric disorders: Hallucinations or Confusion; Nervous system disorders: Seizure, Dysphasia, Tremor, or Headache
Cytokine release syndrome.CTCAE v5.0 Change: Addition: Navigational note; Clarification: Grade 1, 2, 3, 4, Definition

Immune system disorders - Other, specify.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Immune system disorders - Other, specify.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Immune system disorders - Other, specify.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Serum sickness.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Serum sickness.Grade 2: Moderate arthralgia; fever, rash, urticaria, antihistamines indicated
Serum sickness.Grade 3: Severe arthralgia or arthritis; extensive rash; steroids or IV fluids indicated
Serum sickness.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; pressor or ventilatory support indicated
Serum sickness.Definition: A disorder characterized by a delayed-type hypersensitivity reaction to foreign proteins derived from an animal serum. It occurs approximately six to twenty-one days following the administration of the foreign antigen. Symptoms include fever, arthralgias, myalgias, skin eruptions, lymphadenopathy, chest marked discomfort and dyspnea.

Abdominal infection.Grade 2: Oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Abdominal infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Abdominal infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the abdominal cavity.

Anorectal infection.Grade 2: Oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Anorectal infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Anorectal infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the anal area and the rectum.

Appendicitis.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Appendicitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by acute inflammation to the vermiform appendix caused by a pathogenic agent.

Appendicitis perforated.Grade 3: Medical intervention indicated; operative intervention indicated
Appendicitis perforated.Definition: A disorder characterized by acute inflammation to the vermiform appendix caused by a pathogenic agent with gangrenous changes resulting in the rupture of the appendiceal wall. The appendiceal wall rupture causes the release of inflammatory and bacterial contents from the appendiceal lumen into the abdominal cavity.

Arteritis infective.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Arteritis infective.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving an artery.

Bacteremia.Grade 2: Blood culture positive with no signs or symptoms
Bacteremia.Definition: A disorder characterized by the presence of bacteria in the blood stream.

Biliary tract infection.Grade 2: Oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Biliary tract infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Biliary tract infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the biliary tract.

Bladder infection.Grade 2: Oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Bladder infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Bladder infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the bladder.

Bone infection.Grade 2: Oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Bone infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Bone infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the bones.

Breast infection.Grade 2: Local infection with moderate symptoms; oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Breast infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; severe infection; axillary adenitis
Breast infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the breast.

Bronchial infection.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Bronchial infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Bronchial infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the bronchi.

Catheter related infection.Grade 2: Localized; local intervention indicated; oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Catheter related infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Catheter related infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process that arises secondary to catheter use.

Cecal infection.Grade 2: Localized; oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Cecal infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Cecal infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the cecum.

Cervicitis infection.Grade 2: Localized; local intervention indicated (e.g., topical antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Cervicitis infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Cervicitis infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the uterine cervix.

Conjunctivitis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; intervention not indicated
Conjunctivitis.Grade 2: Symptomatic; moderate decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity 20/40 and better or 3 lines or less decreased vision from known baseline)
Conjunctivitis.Grade 3: Symptomatic with marked decrease in visual acuity (best corrected visual acuity worse than 20/40 or more than 3 lines of decreased vision from known baseline, up to 20/200); limiting self care ADL
Conjunctivitis.Grade 4: Best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye
Conjunctivitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation, swelling and redness to the conjunctiva of the eye.
Conjunctivitis.Navigational Note: Consider Infections and infestations: Conjunctivitis infective if caused by infection
Conjunctivitis.CTCAE v5.0 Change: Addition: Grade 4, Navigational note; Clarification: Grade 2, 3; Primary SOC change

Conjunctivitis infective.Grade 2: Localized; local intervention indicated (e.g., topical antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Conjunctivitis infective.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Conjunctivitis infective.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the conjunctiva. Clinical manifestations include pink or red color in the eyes.

Corneal infection.Grade 2: Localized; local intervention indicated (e.g., topical antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Corneal infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Corneal infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the cornea.

Cranial nerve infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Cranial nerve infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving a cranial nerve.

Cytomegalovirus infection reactivation.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Cytomegalovirus infection reactivation.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; IV intervention indicated
Cytomegalovirus infection reactivation.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated; blindness
Cytomegalovirus infection reactivation.Definition: A disorder characterized by the reactivation of cytomegalovirus (CMV).

Device related infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Device related infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the use of a medical device.

Duodenal infection.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; medical intervention indicated (e.g., oral antibiotics)
Duodenal infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Duodenal infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the duodenum.

Encephalitis infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; severe changes in mental status; self-limited seizure activity; focal neurologic abnormalities
Encephalitis infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the brain tissue.

Encephalomyelitis infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Encephalomyelitis infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the brain and spinal cord tissues.

Endocarditis infective.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Endocarditis infective.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the endocardial layer of the heart.

Endophthalmitis.Grade 4: Best corrected visual acuity of 20/200 or worse in the affected eye
Endophthalmitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the internal structures of the eye.

Enterocolitis infectious.Grade 2: Passage of >3 unformed stools per 24 hrs or duration of illness >48 hrs; moderate abdominal pain; oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Enterocolitis infectious.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated; profuse watery diarrhea with signs of hypovolemia; bloody diarrhea; fever; severe abdominal pain; hospitalization indicated
Enterocolitis infectious.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the small and large intestines.
Enterocolitis infectious.Navigational Note: Includes Clostridium difficile (c. diff, c. difficile).

Epstein-Barr virus infection reactivatio.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Epstein-Barr virus infection reactivatio.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; IV intervention indicated
Epstein-Barr virus infection reactivatio.Definition: A disorder characterized by the reactivation of Epstein-Barr virus (EBV).

Esophageal infection.Grade 2: Local intervention indicated (e.g., oral antibiotic, antifungal, antiviral)
Esophageal infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Esophageal infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the esophagus.

Eye infection.Grade 2: Localized; local intervention indicated (e.g., topical antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Eye infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Eye infection.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated; enucleation
Eye infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the eye.

Folliculitis.Grade 1: Covering <10% of the body surface area; no intervention indicated
Folliculitis.Grade 2: Covering 10-30% of the body surface area; topical intervention initiated
Folliculitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation or infection of the hair follicles.

Fungemia.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated
Fungemia.Definition: A disorder characterized by the presence of fungus in the blood stream.

Gallbladder infection.Grade 2: Oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Gallbladder infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Gallbladder infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the gallbladder.

Gum infection.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Gum infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Gum infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the gums.

Hepatic infection.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Hepatic infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Hepatic infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the liver.

Hepatitis B reactivation.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hepatitis B reactivation.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; IV intervention indicated
Hepatitis B reactivation.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated; severe decompensated liver function (e.g., coagulopathy, encephalopathy, coma)
Hepatitis B reactivation.Definition: A disorder characterized by the reactivation of hepatitis B virus.

Hepatitis viral.Grade 3: Symptomatic liver dysfunction; fibrosis by biopsy; compensated cirrhosis; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated
Hepatitis viral.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; severe decompensated liver function (e.g., coagulopathy, encephalopathy, coma)
Hepatitis viral.Definition: A disorder characterized by a viral pathologic process involving the liver parenchyma.

Herpes simplex reactivation.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Herpes simplex reactivation.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; IV intervention indicated
Herpes simplex reactivation.Definition: A disorder characterized by the reactivation of Herpes simplex virus

Infections and infestations - Other, spe.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Infections and infestations - Other, spe.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Infections and infestations - Other, spe.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Infective myositis.Grade 2: Localized; local intervention indicated (e.g., topical antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Infective myositis.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Infective myositis.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the skeletal muscles.

Joint infection.Grade 2: Localized; local intervention indicated; oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral); needle aspiration indicated (single or multiple)
Joint infection.Grade 3: Arthroscopic intervention indicated (e.g., drainage) or arthrotomy (e.g., open surgical drainage)
Joint infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving a joint.

Kidney infection.Grade 2: Oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Kidney infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Kidney infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the kidney.

Laryngitis.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Laryngitis.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Laryngitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by an inflammatory process involving the larynx.
Laryngitis.Navigational Note: For symptoms and no intervention, consider Respiratory, thoracic and mediastinal disorders: Sore throat or Hoarseness.

Lip infection.Grade 2: Oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Lip infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Lip infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the lips.

Lung infection.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Lung infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Lung infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the lungs, including pneumonia.
Lung infection.Navigational Note: If infection is due to aspiration, consider reporting Respiratory, thoracic and mediastinal disorders: Apiration
Lung infection.CTCAE v5.0 Change: Addition: Navigational note; Clarification: Grade 2, 3, Definition

Lymph gland infection.Grade 2: Localized; oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Lymph gland infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Lymph gland infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the lymph nodes.

Mediastinal infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Mediastinal infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the mediastinum.

Meningitis.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated; focal neurologic deficit
Meningitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by acute inflammation of the meninges of the brain and/or spinal cord.

Mucosal infection.Grade 2: Oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Mucosal infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Mucosal infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving a mucosal surface.

Myelitis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; mild signs (e.g., Babinski's reflex or Lhermitte's sign)
Myelitis.Grade 2: Moderate weakness or sensory loss; limiting instrumental ADL
Myelitis.Grade 3: Severe weakness or sensory loss; limiting self care ADL
Myelitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation involving the spinal cord. Symptoms include weakness, paresthesia, sensory loss, marked discomfort and incontinence.

Nail infection.Grade 2: Oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Nail infection.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Nail infection.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the nail.

Otitis externa.Grade 2: Oral intervention indicated (e.g., antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral)
Otitis externa.Grade 3: IV antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral intervention indicated; invasive intervention indicated
Otitis externa.Definition: A disorder characterized by an infectious process involving the outer ear and ear canal. Contributory factors include excessive water exposure (swimmer's ear infection) and cuts in the ear canal. Symptoms include fullness, itching, swelling and marked discomfort in the ear and ear drainage.
Otitis externa.Navigational Note: Changes associated with radiation to external ear (pinnae) are graded under Injury, poisoning and procedural complications: Dermatitis radiation

Growth suppression.Grade 1: Reduction in growth velocity by 10 - 29% ideally measured over the period of a year
Growth suppression.Grade 2: Reduction in growth velocity by 30 - 49% ideally measured over the period of a year or 0 - 49% reduction in growth from the baseline growth curve
Growth suppression.Grade 3: Reduction in growth velocity of >=50% ideally measured over the period of a year
Growth suppression.Definition: A disorder characterized by stature that is smaller than normal as expected for age.

Head soft tissue necrosis.Grade 2: Local wound care; medical intervention indicated (e.g., dressings or topical medications)
Head soft tissue necrosis.Grade 3: Operative debridement or other invasive intervention indicated (e.g., tissue reconstruction, flap, or grafting)
Head soft tissue necrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a necrotic process occurring in the soft tissues of the head.

Joint effusion.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Joint effusion.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL; invasive intervention indicated
Joint effusion.Definition: A disorder characterized by excessive fluid in a joint, usually as a result of joint inflammation.

Joint range of motion decreased.Grade 1: <=25% loss of ROM (range of motion); decreased ROM limiting athletic activity
Joint range of motion decreased.Grade 2: >25 - 50% decrease in ROM; limiting instrumental ADL
Joint range of motion decreased.Grade 3: >50% decrease in ROM; limiting self care ADL
Joint range of motion decreased.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in joint flexibility of any joint.

Joint range of motion decreased cervical.Grade 1: Mild restriction of rotation or flexion between 60 - 70 degrees
Joint range of motion decreased cervical.Grade 2: Rotation <60 degrees to right or left; <60 degrees of flexion
Joint range of motion decreased cervical.Grade 3: Ankylosed/fused over multiple segments with no C-spine rotation
Joint range of motion decreased cervical.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in flexibility of a cervical spine joint.

Joint range of motion decreased lumbar s.Grade 1: Stiffness; difficulty bending to the floor to pick up a very light object but able to do athletic activity
Joint range of motion decreased lumbar s.Grade 2: Pain with range of motion (ROM) in lumbar spine; requires a reaching aid to pick up a very light object from the floor
Joint range of motion decreased lumbar s.Grade 3: <50% lumbar spine flexion; associated with symptoms of ankylosis or fused over multiple segments with no L-spine flexion (e.g., unable to reach to floor to pick up a very light object)
Joint range of motion decreased lumbar s.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in flexibility of a lumbar spine joint.

Kyphosis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Kyphosis.Grade 3: Severe accentuation; operative intervention indicated; limiting self care ADL
Kyphosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal increase in the curvature of the thoracic portion of the spine.

Lordosis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Lordosis.Grade 3: Severe accentuation; operative intervention indicated; limiting self care ADL
Lordosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal increase in the curvature of the lumbar portion of the spine.

Muscle cramp.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Muscle cramp.Definition: A disorder characterized by marked cramping sensation originating from a muscle or group of muscles.

Muscle weakness lower limb.Grade 1: Symptomatic; perceived by patient but not evident on physical exam
Muscle weakness lower limb.Grade 2: Symptomatic; evident on physical exam; limiting instrumental ADL
Muscle weakness lower limb.Definition: A disorder characterized by a reduction in the strength of the lower limb muscles.

Muscle weakness trunk.Grade 1: Symptomatic; perceived by patient but not evident on physical exam
Muscle weakness trunk.Grade 2: Symptomatic; evident on physical exam; limiting instrumental ADL
Muscle weakness trunk.Definition: A disorder characterized by a reduction in the strength of the trunk muscles.

Muscle weakness upper limb.Grade 1: Symptomatic; perceived by patient but not evident on physical exam
Muscle weakness upper limb.Grade 2: Symptomatic; evident on physical exam; limiting instrumental ADL
Muscle weakness upper limb.Definition: A disorder characterized by a reduction in the strength of the upper limb muscles.

Musculoskeletal and connective tissue di.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Musculoskeletal and connective tissue di.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Musculoskeletal and connective tissue di.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL
