Musculoskeletal deformity.Grade 2: Deformity, hypoplasia, or asymmetry able to be remediated by prosthesis (e.g., shoe insert) or covered by clothing
Musculoskeletal deformity.Grade 3: Significant deformity, hypoplasia, or asymmetry, unable to be remediated by prosthesis or covered by clothing; limiting self care ADL
Musculoskeletal deformity.Definition: A disorder characterized by a malformation of the musculoskeletal system.

Myalgia.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Myalgia.Definition: A disorder characterized by marked discomfort sensation originating from a muscle or group of muscles.

Myositis.Grade 2: Moderate pain associated with weakness; pain limiting instrumental ADL
Myositis.Grade 3: Pain associated with severe weakness; limiting self care ADL
Myositis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation involving the skeletal muscles.

Neck pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Neck pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the neck area.

Neck soft tissue necrosis.Grade 2: Local wound care; medical intervention indicated (e.g., dressings or topical medications)
Neck soft tissue necrosis.Grade 3: Operative debridement or other invasive intervention indicated (e.g., tissue reconstruction, flap, or grafting)
Neck soft tissue necrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a necrotic process occurring in the soft tissues of the neck.

Osteonecrosis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Osteonecrosis.Grade 2: Symptomatic; medical intervention indicated (e.g., analgesics or bisphosphonates); limiting instrumental ADL
Osteonecrosis.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL; elective operative intervention indicated
Osteonecrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by necrotic changes in the bone tissue due to interruption of blood supply. Most often affecting the epiphysis of the long bones, the necrotic changes result in the collapse and the destruction of the bone structure.

Osteonecrosis of jaw.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Osteonecrosis of jaw.Grade 2: Symptomatic; medical intervention indicated (e.g., topical agents); limiting instrumental ADL
Osteonecrosis of jaw.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL; elective operative intervention indicated
Osteonecrosis of jaw.Definition: A disorder characterized by a necrotic process occurring in the bone of the mandible.

Osteoporosis.Grade 1:
Adult: Radiologic evidence of osteoporosis or Bone Mineral Density (BMD) t-score -1 to -2.5 (osteopenia); 

Pediatric: Radiologic evidence of low BMD with z score of <= -2.0 and no history of significant fractures
Osteoporosis.Grade 2:
Adult: BMD t-score < -2.5; loss of height <2 cm; therapy to improve BMD indicated; limiting instrumental ADL;

Pediatric: Low BMD (z-score <= -2.0) and significant fracture history (defined as a long bone fracture of the lower extremity, vertebral compression, 2 or more long bone fractures of the upper extremities); therapy to improve BMD indicated
Osteoporosis.Grade 3:
Adult: Loss of height >=2 cm; hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL; 

Pediatric: Limiting self care ADL
Osteoporosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by reduced bone mass, with a decrease in cortical thickness and in the number and size of the trabeculae of cancellous bone (but normal chemical composition), resulting in increased fracture incidence.

Pain in extremity.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Pain in extremity.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the upper or lower extremities.

Pelvic soft tissue necrosis.Grade 2: Local wound care; medical intervention indicated (e.g., dressings or topical medications)
Pelvic soft tissue necrosis.Grade 3: Operative debridement or other invasive intervention indicated (e.g., tissue reconstruction, flap, or grafting)
Pelvic soft tissue necrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a necrotic process occurring in the soft tissues of the pelvis.

Rhabdomyolysis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic, intervention not indicated; laboratory findings only
Rhabdomyolysis.Definition: A disorder characterized by the breakdown of muscle tissue resulting in the release of muscle fiber contents into the bloodstream.

Rotator cuff injury.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Rotator cuff injury.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Rotator cuff injury.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL
Rotator cuff injury.Definition: A disorder characterized by an injury of the rotator cuff.

Scoliosis.Grade 2: >20 - 45 degrees; visible by forward flexion; limiting instrumental ADL
Scoliosis.Grade 3: >45 degrees; scapular prominence in forward flexion; operative intervention indicated; limiting self care ADL
Scoliosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a malformed, lateral curvature of the spine.

Soft tissue necrosis lower limb.Grade 2: Local wound care; medical intervention indicated (e.g., dressings or topical medications)
Soft tissue necrosis lower limb.Grade 3: Operative debridement or other invasive intervention indicated (e.g., tissue reconstruction, flap, or grafting)
Soft tissue necrosis lower limb.Definition: A disorder characterized by a necrotic process occurring in the soft tissues of the lower extremity.

Soft tissue necrosis upper limb.Grade 2: Local wound care; medical intervention indicated (e.g., dressings or topical medications)
Soft tissue necrosis upper limb.Grade 3: Operative debridement or other invasive intervention indicated (e.g., tissue reconstruction, flap, or grafting)
Soft tissue necrosis upper limb.Definition: A disorder characterized by a necrotic process occurring in the soft tissues of the upper extremity.

Superficial soft tissue fibrosis.Grade 1: Mild induration, able to move skin parallel to plane (sliding) and perpendicular to skin (pinching up)
Superficial soft tissue fibrosis.Grade 2: Moderate induration, able to slide skin, unable to pinch skin; limiting instrumental ADL
Superficial soft tissue fibrosis.Grade 3: Severe induration; unable to slide or pinch skin; limiting joint or orifice movement (e.g., mouth, anus); limiting self care ADL
Superficial soft tissue fibrosis.Grade 4: Generalized; associated with signs or symptoms of impaired breathing or feeding
Superficial soft tissue fibrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by fibrotic degeneration of the superficial soft tissues.

Trismus.Grade 1: Decreased ROM (range of motion) without impaired eating
Trismus.Grade 2: Decreased ROM requiring small bites, soft foods or purees
Trismus.Grade 3: Decreased ROM with inability to adequately aliment or hydrate orally
Trismus.Definition: A disorder characterized by lack of ability to open the mouth fully due to a decrease in the range of motion of the muscles of mastication.

Unequal limb length.Grade 2: Moderate length discrepancy 2 - 5 cm; shoe lift indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Unequal limb length.Grade 3: Severe length discrepancy >5 cm; limiting self care ADL; operative intervention indicated
Unequal limb length.Definition: A disorder characterized by a discrepancy between the lengths of the lower or upper extremities.

Leukemia secondary to oncology chemother.Definition: A disorder characterized by leukemia arising as a result of the mutagenic effect of chemotherapy agents.

Myelodysplastic syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by insufficiently healthy hematapoietic cell production by the bone marrow.

Neoplasms benign, malignant and unspecif.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Neoplasms benign, malignant and unspecif.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Neoplasms benign, malignant and unspecif.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Skin papilloma.Definition: A disorder characterized by the presence of one or more warts.

Treatment related secondary malignancy.Grade 4: Acute life-threatening secondary malignancy; blast crisis in leukemia
Treatment related secondary malignancy.Definition: A disorder characterized by development of a malignancy most probably as a result of treatment for a previously existing malignancy.

Tumor hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Tumor hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding in a tumor.

Tumor pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Tumor pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort from a neoplasm that may be pressing on a nerve, blocking blood vessels, inflamed or fractured from metastasis.

Abducens nerve disorder.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Abducens nerve disorder.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Abducens nerve disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by dysfunction of the abducens nerve (sixth cranial nerve).

Accessory nerve disorder.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Accessory nerve disorder.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Accessory nerve disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by dysfunction of the accessory nerve (eleventh cranial nerve).

Acoustic nerve disorder NOS.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Acoustic nerve disorder NOS.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Acoustic nerve disorder NOS.Definition: A disorder characterized by dysfunction of the acoustic nerve (eighth cranial nerve).

Akathisia.Grade 1: Mild restlessness or increased motor activity
Akathisia.Grade 2: Moderate restlessness or increased motor activity; limiting instrumental ADL
Akathisia.Grade 3: Severe restlessness or increased motor activity; limiting self care ADL
Akathisia.Definition: A disorder characterized by an uncomfortable feeling of inner restlessness and inability to stay still; this is a side effect of some psychotropic drugs.

Amnesia.Grade 2: Moderate; short term memory loss; limiting instrumental ADL
Amnesia.Grade 3: Severe; long term memory loss; limiting self care ADL
Amnesia.Definition: A disorder characterized by systematic and extensive loss of memory.

Anosmia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a change in the sense of smell.

Aphonia.Definition: A disorder characterized by the inability to speak. It may result from injuries to the vocal cords or may be functional (psychogenic).

Arachnoiditis.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Arachnoiditis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the arachnoid membrane and adjacent subarachnoid space.

Ataxia.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Ataxia.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL; mechanical assistance indicated
Ataxia.Definition: A disorder characterized by lack of coordination of muscle movements resulting in the impairment or inability to perform voluntary activities.

Brachial plexopathy.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Brachial plexopathy.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Brachial plexopathy.Definition: A disorder characterized by regional paresthesia of the brachial plexus, marked discomfort and muscle weakness, and limited movement in the arm or hand.

Central nervous system necrosis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Central nervous system necrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a necrotic process occurring in the brain and/or spinal cord.

Cerebrospinal fluid leakage.Grade 1: Post-craniotomy: asymptomatic; Post-lumbar puncture: transient headache; postural care indicated
Cerebrospinal fluid leakage.Grade 2: Post-craniotomy: moderate symptoms; medical intervention indicated; Post-lumbar puncture: persistent moderate symptoms; blood patch indicated
Cerebrospinal fluid leakage.Definition: A disorder characterized by loss of cerebrospinal fluid into the surrounding tissues.

Cognitive disturbance.Grade 1: Mild cognitive disability; not interfering with work/school/life performance; specialized educational services/devices not indicated
Cognitive disturbance.Grade 2: Moderate cognitive disability; interfering with work/school/life performance but capable of independent living; specialized resources on part time basis indicated
Cognitive disturbance.Grade 3: Severe cognitive disability; significant impairment of work/school/life performance
Cognitive disturbance.Definition: A disorder characterized by a conspicuous change in cognitive function.

Concentration impairment.Grade 1: Mild inattention or decreased level of concentration
Concentration impairment.Grade 2: Moderate impairment in attention or decreased level of concentration; limiting instrumental ADL
Concentration impairment.Grade 3: Severe impairment in attention or decreased level of concentration; limiting self care ADL
Concentration impairment.Definition: A disorder characterized by a deterioration in the ability to concentrate.

Depressed level of consciousness.Grade 2: Sedation; slow response to stimuli; limiting instrumental ADL
Depressed level of consciousness.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; coma; urgent intervention indicated
Depressed level of consciousness.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in ability to perceive and respond.

Dizziness.Grade 1: Mild unsteadiness or sensation of movement
Dizziness.Grade 2: Moderate unsteadiness or sensation of movement; limiting instrumental ADL
Dizziness.Grade 3: Severe unsteadiness or sensation of movement; limiting self care ADL
Dizziness.Definition: A disorder characterized by a disturbing sensation of lightheadedness, unsteadiness, giddiness, spinning or rocking.

Dysarthria.Grade 2: Moderate impairment of articulation or slurred speech
Dysarthria.Grade 3: Severe impairment of articulation or slurred speech
Dysarthria.Definition: A disorder characterized by slow and slurred speech resulting from an inability to coordinate the muscles used in speech.

Dysesthesia.Grade 2: Moderate sensory alteration; limiting instrumental ADL
Dysesthesia.Grade 3: Severe sensory alteration; limiting self care ADL
Dysesthesia.Definition: A disorder characterized by distortion of sensory perception, resulting in an abnormal and unpleasant sensation.

Dysgeusia.Grade 1: Altered taste but no change in diet
Dysgeusia.Grade 2: Altered taste with change in diet (e.g., oral supplements); noxious or unpleasant taste; loss of taste
Dysgeusia.Definition: A disorder characterized by abnormal sensual experience with the taste of foodstuffs; it can be related to a decrease in the sense of smell.

Dysphasia.Grade 1: Awareness of receptive or expressive characteristics; not impairing ability to communicate
Dysphasia.Grade 2: Moderate receptive or expressive characteristics; impairing ability to communicate spontaneously
Dysphasia.Grade 3: Severe receptive or expressive characteristics; impairing ability to read, write or communicate intelligibly
Dysphasia.Definition: A disorder characterized by impairment of verbal communication skills, often resulting from brain damage.

Edema cerebral.Definition: A disorder characterized by swelling due to an excessive accumulation of fluid in the brain.

Encephalopathy.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Encephalopathy.Definition: A disorder characterized by a pathologic process involving the brain.

Extrapyramidal disorder.Grade 2: Moderate involuntary movements; limiting instrumental ADL
Extrapyramidal disorder.Grade 3: Severe involuntary movements or torticollis; limiting self care ADL
Extrapyramidal disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by abnormal, repetitive, involuntary muscle movements, frenzied speech and extreme restlessness.

Facial muscle weakness.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Facial muscle weakness.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Facial muscle weakness.Definition: A disorder characterized by a reduction in the strength of the facial muscles.

Facial nerve disorder.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Facial nerve disorder.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Facial nerve disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by dysfunction of the facial nerve (seventh cranial nerve).

Glossopharyngeal nerve disorder.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Glossopharyngeal nerve disorder.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Glossopharyngeal nerve disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by dysfunction of the glossopharyngeal nerve (ninth cranial nerve).

Guillain-Barre syndrome.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Guillain-Barre syndrome.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated; intubation
Guillain-Barre syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by the body's immune system attacking the peripheral nervous system causing ascending paralysis.

Headache.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Headache.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in various parts of the head, not confined to the area of distribution of any nerve.

Hydrocephalus.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hydrocephalus.Grade 3: Severe symptoms or neurological deficit; intervention indicated
Hydrocephalus.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal increase of cerebrospinal fluid in the ventricles of the brain.

Hypersomnia.Definition: A disorder characterized by characterized by excessive sleepiness during the daytime.

Hypoglossal nerve disorder.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hypoglossal nerve disorder.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Hypoglossal nerve disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by dysfunction of the hypoglossal nerve (twelfth cranial nerve).

Intracranial hemorrhage.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Intracranial hemorrhage.Grade 3: Ventriculostomy, ICP monitoring, intraventricular thrombolysis, or invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Intracranial hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the cranium.

Ischemia cerebrovascular.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Ischemia cerebrovascular.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease or absence of blood supply to the brain caused by obstruction (thrombosis or embolism) of an artery resulting in neurological damage.
Ischemia cerebrovascular.Navigational Note: Prior to using this term consider Nervous system disorder: TIA or Stroke

Lethargy.Grade 1: Mild symptoms; reduced alertness and awareness
Lethargy.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in consciousness characterized by mental and physical inertness.

Leukoencephalopathy.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; small focal T2/FLAIR hyperintensities; involving periventricular white matter or <1/3 of susceptible areas of cerebrum +/- mild increase in subarachnoid space (SAS) and/or mild ventriculomegaly
Leukoencephalopathy.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; focal T2/FLAIR hyperintensities, involving periventricular white matter extending into centrum semiovale or involving 1/3 to 2/3 of susceptible areas of cerebrum +/- moderate increase in SAS and/or moderate ventriculomegaly
Leukoencephalopathy.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; extensive T2/FLAIR hyperintensities, involving periventricular white matter involving 2/3 or more of susceptible areas of cerebrum +/- moderate to severe increase in SAS and/or moderate to severe ventriculomegaly
Leukoencephalopathy.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; extensive T2/FLAIR hyperintensities, involving periventricular white matter involving most of susceptible areas of cerebrum +/- moderate to severe increase in SAS and/or moderate to severe ventriculomegaly
Leukoencephalopathy.Definition: A disorder characterized by diffuse reactive astrocytosis with multiple areas of necrotic foci without inflammation.

Memory impairment.Grade 2: Moderate memory impairment; limiting instrumental ADL
Memory impairment.Grade 3: Severe memory impairment; limiting self care ADL
Memory impairment.Definition: A disorder characterized by a deterioration in memory function.

Meningismus.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Meningismus.Definition: A disorder characterized by neck stiffness, headache, and photophobia resulting from irritation of the cerebral meninges.

Movements involuntary.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Movements involuntary.Definition: A disorder characterized by uncontrolled and purposeless movements.

Muscle weakness left-sided.Grade 1: Symptomatic; perceived by patient but not evident on physical exam
Muscle weakness left-sided.Grade 2: Symptomatic; evident on physical exam; limiting instrumental ADL
Muscle weakness left-sided.Definition: A disorder characterized by a reduction in the strength of the muscles on the left side of the body.

Muscle weakness right-sided.Grade 1: Symptomatic; perceived by patient but not evident on physical exam
Muscle weakness right-sided.Grade 2: Symptomatic; evident on physical exam; limiting instrumental ADL
Muscle weakness right-sided.Definition: A disorder characterized by a reduction in the strength of the muscles on the right side of the body.

Myasthenia gravis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Myasthenia gravis.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Myasthenia gravis.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL
Myasthenia gravis.Definition: A disorder characterized by weakness and rapid fatigue of any of the skeletal muscles.

Nervous system disorders - Other, specif.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Nervous system disorders - Other, specif.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Nervous system disorders - Other, specif.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Neuralgia.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Neuralgia.Definition: A disorder characterized by intense painful sensation along a nerve or group of nerves.

Nystagmus.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Nystagmus.Definition: A disorder characterized by involuntary movements of the eyeballs.

Oculomotor nerve disorder.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Oculomotor nerve disorder.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Oculomotor nerve disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by dysfunction of the oculomotor nerve (third cranial nerve).

Olfactory nerve disorder.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Olfactory nerve disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by dysfunction of the olfactory nerve (first cranial nerve).

Paresthesia.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Paresthesia.Definition: A disorder characterized by functional disturbances of sensory neurons resulting in abnormal cutaneous sensations of tingling, numbness, pressure, cold, and/or warmth.

Peripheral motor neuropathy.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only
Peripheral motor neuropathy.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Peripheral motor neuropathy.Definition: A disorder characterized by damage or dysfunction of the peripheral motor nerves.
Peripheral motor neuropathy.Navigational Note: Also consider Nervous system disroders: Peripheral sensory neuropathy
Peripheral motor neuropathy.CTCAE v5.0 Change: Addition: Navigational note; Clarification: Grade 1, 3, Definition

Peripheral sensory neuropathy.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Peripheral sensory neuropathy.Definition: A disorder characterized by damage or dysfunction of the peripheral sensory nerves.

Phantom pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Phantom pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort related to a limb or an organ that is removed from or is not physically part of the body.

Presyncope.Definition: A disorder characterized by an episode of lightheadedness and dizziness which may precede an episode of syncope.

Pyramidal tract syndrome.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Pyramidal tract syndrome.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Pyramidal tract syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by dysfunction of the corticospinal (pyramidal) tracts of the spinal cord. Symptoms include an increase in the muscle tone in the lower extremities, hyperreflexia, positive Babinski and a decrease in fine motor coordination.

Radiculitis.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Radiculitis.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Radiculitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation involving a nerve root. Patients experience marked discomfort radiating along a nerve path because of spinal pressure on the connecting nerve root.

Recurrent laryngeal nerve palsy.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Recurrent laryngeal nerve palsy.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; medical intervention indicated (e.g., thyroplasty, vocal cord injection)
Recurrent laryngeal nerve palsy.Definition: A disorder characterized by paralysis of the recurrent laryngeal nerve.

Reversible posterior leukoencephalopathy.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL; hospitalization
Reversible posterior leukoencephalopathy.Definition: A disorder characterized by headaches, mental status changes, visual disturbances, and/or seizures associated with imaging findings of posterior leukoencephalopathy. It has been observed in association with hypertensive encephalopathy, eclampsia, and immunosuppressive and cytotoxic drug treatment. It is an acute or subacute reversible condition. Also known as posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (PRES).

Seizure.Grade 1: Brief partial seizure and no loss of consciousness
Seizure.Grade 3: New onset seizures (partial or generalized); multiple seizures despite medical intervention
Seizure.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sudden, involuntary skeletal muscular contractions of cerebral or brain stem origin.

Somnolence.Grade 1: Mild but more than usual drowsiness or sleepiness
Somnolence.Definition: A disorder characterized by characterized by excessive sleepiness and drowsiness.

Spasticity.Grade 1: Mild or slight increase in muscle tone
Spasticity.Grade 2: Moderate increase in muscle tone and increase in resistance through range of motion
Spasticity.Grade 3: Severe increase in muscle tone and increase in resistance through range of motion
Spasticity.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; unable to move active or passive range of motion
Spasticity.Definition: A disorder characterized by increased involuntary muscle tone that affects the regions interfering with voluntary movement. It results in gait, movement, and speech disturbances.

Spinal cord compression.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Spinal cord compression.Definition: A disorder characterized by pressure on the spinal cord.

Stroke.Grade 2: Mild to moderate neurologic deficit; limiting instrumental ADL
Stroke.Grade 3: Severe neurologic deficit; limiting self care ADL; hospitalization
Stroke.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease or absence of blood supply to the brain caused by obstruction (thrombosis or embolism) of an artery resulting in neurological damage.

Syncope.Definition: A disorder characterized by spontaneous loss of consciousness caused by insufficient blood supply to the brain.

Tendon reflex decreased.Grade 2: Ankle reflex absent; other reflexes reduced
Tendon reflex decreased.Definition: A disorder characterized by less than normal deep tendon reflexes.
Tendon reflex decreased.Navigational Note: Also consider Nervous system disorders: Peripheral motor neuropathy or Peripheral sensory neuropathy

Transient ischemic attacks.Grade 1: Mild neurologic deficit with or without imaging confirmation
Transient ischemic attacks.Grade 2: Moderate neurologic deficit with or without imaging confirmation
Transient ischemic attacks.Definition: A disorder characterized by a brief attack (less than 24 hours) of cerebral dysfunction of vascular origin, with no persistent neurological deficit.
Transient ischemic attacks.Navigational Note: If >24 hours, Consider Nervous system disorders: Stroke

Tremor.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Tremor.Definition: A disorder characterized by the uncontrolled shaking movement of the whole body or individual parts.

Trigeminal nerve disorder.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Trigeminal nerve disorder.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Trigeminal nerve disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by dysfunction of the trigeminal nerve (fifth cranial nerve).

Trochlear nerve disorder.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Trochlear nerve disorder.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Trochlear nerve disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by dysfunction of the trochlear nerve (fourth cranial nerve).

Vagus nerve disorder.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Vagus nerve disorder.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Vagus nerve disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by dysfunction of the vagus nerve (tenth cranial nerve).

Vasovagal reaction.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sudden drop of the blood pressure, bradycardia, and peripheral vasodilation that may lead to loss of consciousness. It results from an increase in the stimulation of the vagus nerve.

Fetal growth retardation.Grade 2: <10% percentile of weight for gestational age
Fetal growth retardation.Grade 3: <5% percentile of weight for gestational age
Fetal growth retardation.Grade 4: <1% percentile of weight for gestational age
Fetal growth retardation.Definition: A disorder characterized by inhibition of fetal growth resulting in the inability of the fetus to achieve its potential weight.

Pregnancy loss.Grade 4: Fetal loss at any gestational age

Pregnancy, puerperium and perinatal cond.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Pregnancy, puerperium and perinatal cond.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Pregnancy, puerperium and perinatal cond.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Premature delivery.Grade 1: Delivery of a liveborn infant at >34 to 37 weeks gestation
Premature delivery.Grade 2: Delivery of a liveborn infant at >28 to 34 weeks gestation
Premature delivery.Grade 3: Delivery of a liveborn infant at 24 to 28 weeks gestation
Premature delivery.Grade 4: Delivery of a liveborn infant at 24 weeks of gestation or less
Premature delivery.Definition: A disorder characterized by delivery of a viable infant before the normal end of gestation. Typically, viability is achievable between the twentieth and thirty-seventh week of gestation.

Agitation.Definition: A disorder characterized by a state of restlessness associated with unpleasant feelings of irritability and tension.

Anorgasmia.Grade 1: Inability to achieve orgasm not adversely affecting relationship
Anorgasmia.Grade 2: Inability to achieve orgasm adversely affecting relationship
Anorgasmia.Definition: A disorder characterized by an inability to achieve orgasm.

Anxiety.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL; hospitalization indicated
Anxiety.Definition: A disorder characterized by apprehension of danger and dread accompanied by restlessness, tension, tachycardia, and dyspnea unattached to a clearly identifiable stimulus.

Confusion.Grade 3: Severe disorientation; limiting self care ADL
Confusion.Definition: A disorder characterized by a lack of clear and orderly thought and behavior.

Delayed orgasm.Grade 1: Delay in achieving orgasm not adversely affecting relationship
Delayed orgasm.Grade 2: Delay in achieving orgasm adversely affecting relationship
Delayed orgasm.Definition: A disorder characterized by sexual dysfunction characterized by a delay in climax.

Delirium.Grade 2: Moderate and acute confusional state; limiting instrumental ADL
Delirium.Grade 3: Severe and acute confusional state; limiting self care ADL; urgent intervention indicated; new onset
Delirium.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences, threats of harm to self or others; urgent intervention indicated
Delirium.Definition: A disorder characterized by the acute and sudden development of confusion, illusions, movement changes, inattentiveness, agitation, and hallucinations. Usually, it is a reversible condition.

Delusions.Grade 3: Severe delusional symptoms; hospitalization not indicated; new onset
Delusions.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences, threats of harm to self or others; hospitalization indicated
Delusions.Definition: A disorder characterized by false personal beliefs held contrary to reality, despite contradictory evidence and common sense.

Depression.Grade 2: Moderate depressive symptoms; limiting instrumental ADL
Depression.Grade 3: Severe depressive symptoms; limiting self care ADL; hospitalization not indicated
Depression.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences, threats of harm to self or others; hospitalization indicated
Depression.Definition: A disorder characterized by melancholic feelings of grief or unhappiness.

Euphoria.Definition: A disorder characterized by an exaggerated feeling of well-being which is disproportionate to events and stimuli.

Hallucinations.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences, threats of harm to self or others; hospitalization indicated
Hallucinations.Definition: A disorder characterized by a false sensory perception in the absence of an external stimulus.

Insomnia.Grade 1: Mild difficulty falling asleep, staying asleep or waking up early
Insomnia.Grade 2: Moderate difficulty falling asleep, staying asleep or waking up early
Insomnia.Grade 3: Severe difficulty in falling asleep, staying asleep or waking up early
Insomnia.Definition: A disorder characterized by difficulty in falling asleep and/or remaining asleep.

Irritability.Grade 2: Moderate; limiting instrumental ADL; increased attention indicated
Irritability.Grade 3: Severe abnormal or excessive response; limiting self care ADL; inconsolable; medical or psychiatric intervention indicated
Irritability.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal responsiveness to stimuli or physiological arousal; may be in response to pain, fright, a drug, an emotional situation or a medical condition.

Libido decreased.Grade 1: Decrease in sexual interest not adversely affecting relationship
Libido decreased.Grade 2: Decrease in sexual interest adversely affecting relationship
Libido decreased.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in sexual desire.

Libido increased.Definition: A disorder characterized by an increase in sexual desire.

Mania.Grade 1: Mild manic symptoms (e.g., elevated mood, rapid thoughts, rapid speech, decreased need for sleep)
Mania.Grade 2: Moderate manic symptoms (e.g., relationship and work difficulties; poor hygiene)
Mania.Grade 3: Severe manic symptoms (e.g., hypomania; major sexual or financial indiscretions); hospitalization not indicated; new onset
Mania.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences, threats of harm to self or others; hospitalization indicated
Mania.Definition: A disorder characterized by excitement of psychotic proportions manifested by mental and physical hyperactivity, disorganization of behavior and elevation of mood.

Personality change.Grade 3: Severe personality change; hospitalization not indicated
Personality change.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences, threats of harm to self or others; hospitalization indicated
Personality change.Definition: A disorder characterized by a conspicuous change in a person's behavior and thinking.

Psychiatric disorders - Other, specify.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Psychiatric disorders - Other, specify.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Psychiatric disorders - Other, specify.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; limiting self care ADL
Psychiatric disorders - Other, specify.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; hospitalization or urgent intervention indicated

Psychosis.Grade 2: Moderate psychotic symptoms (e.g., disorganized speech; impaired reality testing)
Psychosis.Grade 3: Severe psychotic symptoms (e.g., paranoid, extreme disorganization); hospitalization not indicated; new onset
Psychosis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences, threats of harm to self or others; hospitalization indicated
Psychosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by personality change, impaired functioning, and loss of touch with reality. It may be a manifestation of schizophrenia, bipolar disorder or brain tumor.

Restlessness.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Restlessness.Definition: A disorder characterized by an inability to rest, relax or be still.

Suicidal ideation.Grade 1: Increased thoughts of death but no wish to kill oneself
Suicidal ideation.Grade 2: Suicidal ideation with no specific plan or intent
Suicidal ideation.Grade 3: Specific plan to commit suicide without serious intent to die which may not require hospitalization
Suicidal ideation.Grade 4: Specific plan to commit suicide with serious intent to die which requires hospitalization
Suicidal ideation.Definition: A disorder characterized by thoughts of taking one's own life.

Suicide attempt.Grade 3: Suicide attempt or gesture without intent to die
Suicide attempt.Grade 4: Suicide attempt with intent to die which requires hospitalization
Suicide attempt.Definition: A disorder characterized by self-inflicted harm in an attempt to end one's own life.

Acute kidney injury.Definition: A disorder characterized by the acute loss of renal function (within 2 weeks) and is traditionally classified as pre-renal (low blood flow into kidney), renal (kidney damage) and post-renal causes (ureteral or bladder outflow obstruction).
Acute kidney injury.CTCAE v5.0 Change: Addition: Navigational note; Clarification: Grade 3, Definition; Deletion: Grade 1, 2

Bladder perforation.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; organ failure; urgent operative intervention indicated
Bladder perforation.Definition: A disorder characterized by a rupture in the bladder wall.

Bladder spasm.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sudden and involuntary contraction of the bladder wall.

Chronic kidney disease.Grade 1: eGFR (estimated Glomerular Filtration Rate) or CrCl (creatinine clearance) <LLN - 60 ml/min/1.73 m2 or proteinuria 2+ present; urine protein/creatinine >0.5
Chronic kidney disease.Grade 4: eGFR or CrCl <15 ml/min/1.73 m2; dialysis or renal transplant indicated
Chronic kidney disease.Definition: A disorder characterized by gradual and usually permanent loss of kidney function resulting in renal failure.

Cystitis noninfective.Grade 1: Microscopic hematuria; minimal increase in frequency, urgency, dysuria, or nocturia; new onset of incontinence
Cystitis noninfective.Grade 2: Moderate hematuria; moderate increase in frequency, urgency, dysuria, nocturia or incontinence; urinary catheter placement or bladder irrigation indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Cystitis noninfective.Grade 3: Gross hematuria; transfusion, IV medications, or hospitalization indicated; elective invasive intervention indicated
Cystitis noninfective.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent invasive intervention indicated
Cystitis noninfective.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the bladder which is not caused by an infection of the urinary tract.

Dysuria.Definition: A disorder characterized by painful urination.
Dysuria.Navigational Note: If associated with an infection, report the infection. For grades higher than Grade 1, consider Renal and urinary disorders: Bladder spasm or Cystitis noninfective; Infections and infestations: Urinary tract infection.

Glucosuria.Definition: A disorder characterized by laboratory test results that indicate glucose in the urine.

Hematuria.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hematuria.Grade 2: Symptomatic; urinary catheter or bladder irrigation indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Hematuria.Grade 3: Gross hematuria; transfusion, IV medications, or hospitalization indicated; elective invasive intervention indicated; limiting self care ADL
Hematuria.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent invasive intervention indicated
Hematuria.Definition: A disorder characterized by laboratory test results that indicate blood in the urine.

Hemoglobinuria.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hemoglobinuria.Definition: A disorder characterized by laboratory test results that indicate the presence of free hemoglobin in the urine.

Nephrotic syndrome.Grade 3: Not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL
Nephrotic syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by symptoms that include severe edema, proteinuria, and hypoalbuminemia; it is indicative of renal dysfunction.

Proteinuria.Grade 2:
Adult: 2+ and 3+ proteinuria; urinary protein 1.0 - <3.5 g/24 hrs; 

Pediatric: Urine P/C (Protein/Creatinine) ratio 0.5 - 1.9
Proteinuria.Grade 3:
Adult: Urinary protein >=3.5 g/24 hrs; 4+ proteinuria;

Pediatric: Urine P/C (Protein/Creatinine) ratio >1.9
Proteinuria.Definition: A disorder characterized by laboratory test results that indicate the presence of excessive protein in the urine. It is predominantly albumin, but also globulin.
Proteinuria.Navigational Note: 24-hour urine collection takes precedence over dipstick

Renal and urinary disorders - Other, spe.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Renal and urinary disorders - Other, spe.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Renal and urinary disorders - Other, spe.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Renal calculi.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; occasional use of nonprescription analgesics indicated
Renal calculi.Grade 2: Symptomatic; oral antiemetics indicated; around the clock nonprescription analgesics or any oral narcotic analgesics indicated
Renal calculi.Grade 3: Hospitalization indicated; IV intervention (e.g., analgesics, antiemetics); elective invasive intervention indicated
Renal calculi.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent invasive intervention indicated
Renal calculi.Definition: A disorder characterized by the formation of crystals/kidney stones in the pelvis of the kidney.

Renal colic.Grade 1: Mild pain not interfering with activity; nonprescription medication indicated
Renal colic.Grade 2: Moderate pain; limiting instrumental ADL; prescription medication indicated
Renal colic.Grade 3: Hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL
Renal colic.Definition: A disorder characterized by paroxysmal and severe flank marked discomfort radiating to the inguinal area. Often, the cause is the passage of crystals/kidney stones.

Renal hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Renal hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the kidney.

Urinary fistula.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent invasive intervention indicated
Urinary fistula.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal communication between any part of the urinary system and another organ or anatomic site.

Urinary frequency.Grade 2: Limiting instrumental ADL; medical management indicated
Urinary frequency.Definition: A disorder characterized by urination at short intervals.

Urinary incontinence.Grade 1: Occasional (e.g., with coughing, sneezing, etc.), pads not indicated
Urinary incontinence.Grade 2: Spontaneous; pads indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Urinary incontinence.Grade 3: Intervention indicated (e.g., clamp, collagen injections); operative intervention indicated; limiting self care ADL
Urinary incontinence.Definition: A disorder characterized by inability to control the flow of urine from the bladder.

Urinary retention.Grade 1: Urinary, suprapubic or intermittent catheter placement not indicated; able to void with some residual
Urinary retention.Grade 2: Placement of urinary, suprapubic or intermittent catheter placement indicated; medication indicated
Urinary retention.Grade 3: Elective invasive intervention indicated; substantial loss of affected kidney function or mass
Urinary retention.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; organ failure; urgent operative intervention indicated
Urinary retention.Definition: A disorder characterized by accumulation of urine within the bladder because of the inability to urinate.

Urinary tract obstruction.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Urinary tract obstruction.Grade 2: Symptomatic but no hydronephrosis, sepsis, or renal dysfunction; urethral dilation, urinary or suprapubic catheter indicated
Urinary tract obstruction.Grade 3: Altered organ function (e.g., hydronephrosis or renal dysfunction); invasive intervention indicated
Urinary tract obstruction.Definition: A disorder characterized by blockage of the normal flow of contents of the urinary tract.

Urinary tract pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Urinary tract pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the urinary tract.

Urinary urgency.Grade 2: Limiting instrumental ADL; medical management indicated
Urinary urgency.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sudden compelling urge to urinate.

Urine discoloration.Definition: A disorder characterized by a change in the color of the urine.

Amenorrhea.Definition: A disorder characterized by the abnormal absence of menses for at least three consecutive menstrual cycles

Azoospermia.Definition: A disorder characterized by laboratory test results that indicate complete absence of spermatozoa in the semen.

Breast atrophy.Grade 3: Asymmetry >1/3 of breast volume; severe atrophy
Breast atrophy.Definition: A disorder characterized by underdevelopment of the breast.

Breast pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Breast pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the breast region.

Dysmenorrhea.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Dysmenorrhea.Definition: A disorder characterized by abnormally painful abdominal cramps during menses.

Dyspareunia.Grade 1: Mild discomfort or pain associated with vaginal penetration; discomfort relieved with use of vaginal lubricants or estrogen
Dyspareunia.Grade 2: Moderate discomfort or pain associated with vaginal penetration; discomfort or pain partially relieved with use of vaginal lubricants or estrogen
Dyspareunia.Grade 3: Severe discomfort or pain associated with vaginal penetration; discomfort or pain unrelieved by vaginal lubricants or estrogen
Dyspareunia.Definition: A disorder characterized by painful or difficult coitus.

Ejaculation disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by problems related to ejaculation. This category includes premature, delayed, retrograde and painful ejaculation.

Erectile dysfunction.Grade 1: Decrease in erectile function (frequency or rigidity of erections) but intervention not indicated (e.g., medication or use of mechanical device, penile pump)
Erectile dysfunction.Grade 2: Decrease in erectile function (frequency/rigidity of erections), erectile intervention indicated, (e.g., medication or mechanical devices such as penile pump)
Erectile dysfunction.Grade 3: Decrease in erectile function (frequency/rigidity of erections) but erectile intervention not helpful (e.g., medication or mechanical devices such as penile pump); placement of a permanent penile prosthesis indicated (not previously present)
Erectile dysfunction.Definition: A disorder characterized by the persistent or recurrent inability to achieve or to maintain an erection during sexual activity.

Fallopian tube obstruction.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Fallopian tube obstruction.Definition: A disorder characterized by blockage of the normal flow of the contents in the fallopian tube.

Feminization acquired.Definition: A disorder characterized by the development of secondary female sex characteristics in males due to extrinsic factors.

Genital edema.Grade 1: Mild swelling or obscuration of anatomic architecture on close inspection
Genital edema.Grade 2: Readily apparent obscuration of anatomic architecture; obliteration of skin folds; readily apparent deviation from normal anatomic contour
Genital edema.Grade 3: Lymphorrhea; gross deviation from normal anatomic contour; limiting self care ADL
Genital edema.Definition: A disorder characterized by swelling due to an excessive accumulation of fluid in the genitals.

Gynecomastia.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; elective operative intervention indicated
Gynecomastia.Definition: A disorder characterized by excessive development of the breasts in males.

Hematosalpinx.Grade 1: Minimal bleeding identified on imaging study or laparoscopy; intervention not indicated
Hematosalpinx.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent operative intervention indicated
Hematosalpinx.Definition: A disorder characterized by the presence of blood in a fallopian tube.

Irregular menstruation.Grade 1: Intermittent/irregular menses for no more than 3 consecutive menstrual cycles
Irregular menstruation.Grade 2: Intermittent/irregular menses for more than 3 consecutive menstrual cycles
Irregular menstruation.Definition: A disorder characterized by a change in cycle or duration of menses from baseline.
Irregular menstruation.Navigational Note: Also consider Reproductive system and breast disorders: Premature menopause, Amenorrhea.
Irregular menstruation.CTCAE v5.0 Change: Addition: Navigational note; Clarification: Grade 1, 2, Definition; Deletion: Grade 3

Lactation disorder.Grade 1: Mild changes in lactation, not significantly affecting production or expression of breast milk
Lactation disorder.Grade 2: Changes in lactation, significantly affecting breast production or expression of breast milk
Lactation disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by disturbances of milk secretion. It is not necessarily related to pregnancy that is observed in females and can be observed in males.

Menorrhagia.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; medical intervention indicated (e.g., hormones)
Menorrhagia.Grade 3: Severe; transfusion indicated; operative intervention indicated (e.g., hysterectomy)
Menorrhagia.Definition: A disorder characterized by abnormally heavy vaginal bleeding during menses.

Nipple deformity.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; asymmetry with slight retraction and/or thickening of the nipple areolar complex
Nipple deformity.Grade 2: Symptomatic; asymmetry of nipple areolar complex with moderate retraction and/or thickening of the nipple areolar complex
Nipple deformity.Definition: A disorder characterized by a malformation of the nipple.

Oligospermia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in the number of spermatozoa in the semen.

Ovarian hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Ovarian hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the ovary.

Ovarian rupture.Grade 1: Asymptomatic clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Ovarian rupture.Definition: A disorder characterized by tearing or disruption of the ovarian tissue.

Ovulation pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in one side of the abdomen between menstrual cycles, around the time of the discharge of the ovum from the ovarian follicle.

Pelvic floor muscle weakness.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Pelvic floor muscle weakness.Grade 2: Symptomatic, not interfering with bladder, bowel, or vaginal function; limiting instrumental ADL
Pelvic floor muscle weakness.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Pelvic floor muscle weakness.Definition: A disorder characterized by a reduction in the strength of the muscles of the pelvic floor.

Pelvic pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Pelvic pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the pelvis.

Penile pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Penile pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the penis.

Perineal pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Perineal pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the area between the genital organs and the anus.

Premature menopause.Definition: A disorder characterized by premature ovarian failure. Symptoms may include hot flashes, night sweats, mood swings, and a decrease in sex drive. Laboratory findings include elevated luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH.)
Premature menopause.CTCAE v5.0 Change: Addition: Grade 2; Clarification: Definition; Deletion: Grade 3

Prostatic hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Prostatic hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the prostate gland.

Prostatic obstruction.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Prostatic obstruction.Definition: A disorder characterized by compression of the urethra secondary to enlargement of the prostate gland. This results in voiding difficulties (straining to void, slow urine stream, and incomplete emptying of the bladder).

Prostatic pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Prostatic pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the prostate gland.

Reproductive system and breast disorders.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Reproductive system and breast disorders.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Reproductive system and breast disorders.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Scrotal pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Scrotal pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the scrotal area.

Spermatic cord hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Spermatic cord hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the spermatic cord.

Spermatic cord obstruction.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Spermatic cord obstruction.Definition: A disorder characterized by blockage of the normal flow of the contents of the spermatic cord.

Testicular disorder.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Testicular disorder.Grade 2: Symptomatic but not interfering with sexual function; intervention not indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Testicular disorder.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; interfering with sexual function; limiting self care ADL; intervention indicated
Testicular disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by abnormal function or appearance of the testis.
Testicular disorder.Navigational Note: Also consider Reproductive system and breast disorders: Genital edema or other AE terms in the Renal and urinary disorders SOC or Reproductive system and breast disorders SOC.
Testicular disorder.CTCAE v5.0 Change: Addition: Navigational note; Clarification: Grade 2, 3, Definition; Deletion: Grade 5

Testicular hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Testicular hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the testis.

Testicular pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Testicular pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the testis.

Uterine fistula.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal communication between the uterus and another organ or anatomic site.

Uterine hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Uterine hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the uterus.

Uterine obstruction.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Uterine obstruction.Definition: A disorder characterized by blockage of the uterine outlet.

Uterine pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Uterine pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the uterus.

Vaginal discharge.Grade 1: Mild vaginal discharge (greater than baseline for patient)
Vaginal discharge.Grade 2: Moderate to heavy vaginal discharge; use of perineal pad or tampon indicated
Vaginal discharge.Definition: A disorder characterized by vaginal secretions. Mucus produced by the cervical glands is discharged from the vagina naturally, especially during the childbearing years.

Vaginal dryness.Grade 1: Mild vaginal dryness not interfering with sexual function
Vaginal dryness.Grade 2: Moderate vaginal dryness interfering with sexual function or causing frequent discomfort
Vaginal dryness.Grade 3: Severe vaginal dryness resulting in dyspareunia or severe discomfort
Vaginal dryness.Definition: A disorder characterized by an uncomfortable feeling of itching and burning in the vagina.

Vaginal fistula.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal communication between the vagina and another organ or anatomic site.

Vaginal hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Vaginal hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the vagina.

Vaginal inflammation.Grade 1: Mild discomfort or pain, edema, or redness
Vaginal inflammation.Grade 2: Moderate discomfort or pain, edema, or redness; limiting instrumental ADL
Vaginal inflammation.Grade 3: Severe discomfort or pain, edema, or redness; limiting self care ADL; small areas of mucosal ulceration
Vaginal inflammation.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; widespread areas of mucosal ulceration; urgent intervention indicated
Vaginal inflammation.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation involving the vagina. Symptoms may include redness, edema, marked discomfort and an increase in vaginal discharge.

Vaginal obstruction.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Vaginal obstruction.Definition: A disorder characterized by blockage of vaginal canal.

Vaginal pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Vaginal pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the vagina.

Vaginal perforation.Definition: A disorder characterized by a rupture in the vaginal wall.

Vaginal stricture.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; mild vaginal shortening or narrowing
Vaginal stricture.Grade 2: Vaginal narrowing and/or shortening not interfering with physical examination
Vaginal stricture.Grade 3: Vaginal narrowing and/or shortening interfering with the use of tampons, sexual activity or physical examination
Vaginal stricture.Definition: A disorder characterized by a narrowing of the vaginal canal.

Adult respiratory distress syndrome.Grade 3: Present with radiologic findings; intubation not indicated
Adult respiratory distress syndrome.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory or hemodynamic compromise; intubation or urgent intervention indicated
Adult respiratory distress syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by progressive and life-threatening pulmonary distress in the absence of an underlying pulmonary condition, usually following major trauma or surgery.

Allergic rhinitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by an inflammation of the nasal mucous membranes caused by an IgE-mediated response to external allergens. The inflammation may also involve the mucous membranes of the sinuses, eyes, middle ear, and pharynx. Symptoms include sneezing, nasal congestion, rhinorrhea and itching.

Apnea.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory or hemodynamic compromise; intubation or urgent intervention indicated
Apnea.Definition: A disorder characterized by cessation of breathing.

Aspiration.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Aspiration.Grade 2: Altered eating habits; coughing or choking episodes after eating or swallowing; medical intervention indicated (e.g., suction or oxygen)
Aspiration.Grade 3: Dyspnea and pneumonia symptoms (e.g., aspiration pneumonia); hospitalization indicated; unable to aliment orally
Aspiration.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory or hemodynamic compromise; intubation or urgent intervention indicated
Aspiration.Definition: A disorder characterized by inhalation of solids or liquids into the lungs.

Atelectasis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Atelectasis.Grade 2: Symptomatic (e.g., dyspnea, cough); medical intervention indicated (e.g., chest physiotherapy, suctioning); bronchoscopic suctioning
Atelectasis.Grade 3: Supplemental oxygen indicated; hospitalization or elective operative intervention indicated (e.g., stent, laser)
Atelectasis.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory or hemodynamic compromise; intubation or urgent intervention indicated
Atelectasis.Definition: A disorder characterized by the collapse of part or the entire lung.

Bronchial fistula.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal communication between the bronchus and another organ or anatomic site.

Bronchial obstruction.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Bronchial obstruction.Grade 2: Symptomatic (e.g., mild wheezing); endoscopic evaluation indicated; radiographic evidence of atelectasis/lobar collapse; medical management indicated (e.g., steroids, bronchodilators)
Bronchial obstruction.Grade 3: Shortness of breath with stridor; endoscopic intervention indicated (e.g., laser, stent placement)
Bronchial obstruction.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory or hemodynamic compromise; intubation or urgent intervention indicated
Bronchial obstruction.Definition: A disorder characterized by blockage of a bronchus passage, most often by bronchial secretions and exudates.

Bronchial stricture.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Bronchial stricture.Grade 2: Symptomatic (e.g., rhonchi or wheezing) but without respiratory distress; medical intervention indicated (e.g., steroids, bronchodilators)
Bronchial stricture.Grade 3: Shortness of breath with stridor; endoscopic intervention indicated (e.g., laser, stent placement)
Bronchial stricture.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory or hemodynamic compromise; intubation or urgent intervention indicated
Bronchial stricture.Definition: A disorder characterized by a narrowing of the bronchial tube.

Bronchopleural fistula.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal communication between a bronchus and the pleural cavity.

Bronchopulmonary hemorrhage.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; invasive intervention not indicated
Bronchopulmonary hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Bronchopulmonary hemorrhage.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; intubation or urgent intervention indicated
Bronchopulmonary hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the bronchial wall and/or lung parenchyma.

Bronchospasm.Grade 2: Symptomatic; medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Bronchospasm.Grade 3: Limiting self care ADL; supplemental oxygen indicated
Bronchospasm.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory or hemodynamic compromise; intubation or urgent intervention indicated
Bronchospasm.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sudden contraction of the smooth muscles of the bronchial wall.

Chylothorax.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Chylothorax.Grade 2: Symptomatic; medical intervention indicated (e.g., fat-restricted diet); thoracentesis or tube drainage indicated
Chylothorax.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; elective operative intervention indicated
Chylothorax.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory or hemodynamic compromise; intubation or urgent intervention indicated
Chylothorax.Definition: A disorder characterized by milky pleural effusion (abnormal collection of fluid) resulting from accumulation of lymph fluid in the pleural cavity.

Cough.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms, medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Cough.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Cough.Definition: A disorder characterized by sudden, often repetitive, spasmodic contraction of the thoracic cavity, resulting in violent release of air from the lungs and usually accompanied by a distinctive sound.

Dyspnea.Grade 1: Shortness of breath with moderate exertion
Dyspnea.Grade 2: Shortness of breath with minimal exertion; limiting instrumental ADL
Dyspnea.Grade 3: Shortness of breath at rest; limiting self care ADL
Dyspnea.Definition: A disorder characterized by an uncomfortable sensation of difficulty breathing.

Epistaxis.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; medical intervention indicated (e.g., nasal packing, cauterization; topical vasoconstrictors)
Epistaxis.Grade 3: Transfusion; invasive intervention indicated (e.g., hemostasis of bleeding site)
Epistaxis.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the nose.

Hiccups.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Hiccups.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; interfering with sleep; limiting self care ADL
Hiccups.Definition: A disorder characterized by repeated gulp sounds that result from an involuntary opening and closing of the glottis. This is attributed to a spasm of the diaphragm.

Hoarseness.Grade 1: Mild or intermittent voice change; fully understandable; self-resolves
Hoarseness.Grade 2: Moderate or persistent voice changes; may require occasional repetition but understandable on telephone; medical evaluation indicated
Hoarseness.Grade 3: Severe voice changes including predominantly whispered speech
Hoarseness.Definition: A disorder characterized by harsh and raspy voice arising from or spreading to the larynx.

Hypoxia.Grade 2: Decreased oxygen saturation with exercise (e.g., pulse oximeter <88%); intermittent supplemental oxygen
Hypoxia.Grade 3: Decreased oxygen saturation at rest (e.g., pulse oximeter <88% or PaO2 <=55 mm Hg)
Hypoxia.Grade 4: Life-threatening airway compromise; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., tracheotomy or intubation)
Hypoxia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in the level of oxygen in the body.

Laryngeal edema.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Laryngeal edema.Grade 2: Symptomatic; medical intervention indicated (e.g., dexamethasone, epinephrine, antihistamines)
Laryngeal edema.Grade 4: Life-threatening airway compromise; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., tracheotomy or intubation)
Laryngeal edema.Definition: A disorder characterized by swelling due to an excessive accumulation of fluid in the larynx.

Laryngeal fistula.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal communication between the larynx and another organ or anatomic site.

Laryngeal hemorrhage.Grade 1: Mild cough or trace hemoptysis; laryngoscopic findings
Laryngeal hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Laryngeal hemorrhage.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., tracheotomy or intubation)
Laryngeal hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the larynx.

Laryngeal inflammation.Grade 3: Severe throat pain; endoscopic intervention indicated
Laryngeal inflammation.Definition: A disorder characterized by an inflammation involving the larynx.

Laryngeal mucositis.Grade 1: Endoscopic findings only; mild discomfort with normal intake
Laryngeal mucositis.Grade 2: Moderate pain, analgesics indicated; altered oral intake; limiting instrumental ADL
Laryngeal mucositis.Grade 3: Severe pain; severely altered eating/swallowing; medical intervention indicated
Laryngeal mucositis.Grade 4: Life-threatening airway compromise; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., tracheotomy or intubation)
Laryngeal mucositis.Definition: A disorder characterized by ulceration or inflammation involving the mucous membrane of the larynx.

Laryngeal obstruction.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Laryngeal obstruction.Grade 2: Symptomatic (e.g., noisy airway breathing), no respiratory distress; medical intervention indicated (e.g., steroids); limiting instrumental ADL
Laryngeal obstruction.Grade 3: Limiting self care ADL; stridor; endoscopic intervention indicated (e.g., stent, laser)
Laryngeal obstruction.Definition: A disorder characterized by blockage of the laryngeal airway.

Laryngeal stenosis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Laryngeal stenosis.Grade 2: Symptomatic (e.g., noisy airway breathing), no respiratory distress; medical intervention indicated (e.g., steroids); limiting instrumental ADL
Laryngeal stenosis.Grade 3: Limiting self care ADL; stridor; endoscopic intervention indicated (e.g., stent, laser)
Laryngeal stenosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a narrowing of the laryngeal airway.

Laryngopharyngeal dysesthesia.Grade 1: Mild symptoms; no anxiety; intervention not indicated
Laryngopharyngeal dysesthesia.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; mild anxiety, but no dyspnea; short duration of observation and/or anxiolytic indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Laryngopharyngeal dysesthesia.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; dyspnea and swallowing difficulty; limiting self care ADL
Laryngopharyngeal dysesthesia.Definition: A disorder characterized by an uncomfortable persistent sensation in the area of the laryngopharynx.

Laryngospasm.Grade 3: Recurrent episodes; noninvasive intervention indicated (e.g., breathing technique, pressure point massage)
Laryngospasm.Grade 4: Persistent or severe episodes associated with syncope; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., fiberoptic laryngoscopy, intubation, botox injection)
Laryngospasm.Definition: A disorder characterized by paroxysmal spasmodic muscular contraction of the vocal cords.

Mediastinal hemorrhage.Grade 1: Mild symptoms; intervention not indicated; radiologic evidence only
Mediastinal hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Mediastinal hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the mediastinum.

Nasal congestion.Grade 3: Associated with bloody nasal discharge or epistaxis
Nasal congestion.Definition: A disorder characterized by obstruction of the nasal passage due to mucosal edema.

Oropharyngeal pain.Grade 2: Moderate pain; altered oral intake; non-narcotics initiated; topical analgesics initiated
Oropharyngeal pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; severely altered eating/swallowing; narcotics initiated; requires parenteral nutrition
Oropharyngeal pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the oropharynx.

Pharyngeal fistula.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal communication between the pharynx and another organ or anatomic site.

Pharyngeal hemorrhage.Grade 3: Transfusion indicated; invasive intervention indicated; hospitalization
Pharyngeal hemorrhage.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; intubation or urgent intervention indicated
Pharyngeal hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the pharynx.

Pharyngeal mucositis.Grade 1: Endoscopic findings only; minimal symptoms with normal oral intake; mild pain but analgesics not indicated
Pharyngeal mucositis.Grade 2: Moderate pain, analgesics indicated; altered oral intake; limiting instrumental ADL
Pharyngeal mucositis.Grade 3: Severe pain; unable to adequately aliment or hydrate orally; limiting self care ADL
Pharyngeal mucositis.Definition: A disorder characterized by ulceration or inflammation involving the mucous membrane of the pharynx.

Pharyngeal necrosis.Grade 3: Inability to aliment adequately by GI tract; invasive intervention indicated; tube feeding or TPN indicated
Pharyngeal necrosis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent operative intervention indicated
Pharyngeal necrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a necrotic process occurring in the pharynx.

Pharyngeal stenosis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Pharyngeal stenosis.Grade 2: Symptomatic (e.g., noisy airway breathing), no respiratory distress; medical intervention indicated (e.g., steroids); limiting instrumental ADL
Pharyngeal stenosis.Grade 3: Limiting self care ADL; stridor; endoscopic intervention indicated (e.g., stent, laser)
Pharyngeal stenosis.Grade 4: Life-threatening airway compromise; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., tracheotomy or intubation)
Pharyngeal stenosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a narrowing of the pharyngeal airway.

Pharyngolaryngeal pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Pharyngolaryngeal pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the pharyngolaryngeal region.

Pleural effusion.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Pleural effusion.Grade 2: Symptomatic; intervention indicated (e.g., diuretics or therapeutic thoracentesis)
Pleural effusion.Grade 3: Symptomatic with respiratory distress and hypoxia; operative intervention including chest tube or pleurodesis indicated
Pleural effusion.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory or hemodynamic compromise; intubation or urgent intervention indicated
Pleural effusion.Definition: A disorder characterized by an increase in amounts of fluid within the pleural cavity. Symptoms include shortness of breath, cough and marked chest discomfort.

Pleural hemorrhage.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; mild hemorrhage confirmed by thoracentesis
Pleural hemorrhage.Grade 2: Symptomatic or associated with pneumothorax; chest tube drainage indicated
Pleural hemorrhage.Grade 3: >1000 ml of blood evacuated; persistent bleeding (150-200 ml/hr for 2 - 4 hr); persistent transfusion indicated; elective operative intervention indicated; hospitalization
Pleural hemorrhage.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; intubation or urgent intervention indicated
Pleural hemorrhage.Definition: A disorder characterized by bleeding from the pleural cavity.

Pleuritic pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Pleuritic pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the pleura.

Pneumonitis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Pneumonitis.Grade 2: Symptomatic; medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Pneumonitis.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL; oxygen indicated
Pneumonitis.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory compromise; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., tracheotomy or intubation)
Pneumonitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation focally or diffusely affecting the lung parenchyma.

Pneumothorax.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Pneumothorax.Grade 3: Sclerosis and/or operative intervention indicated; hospitalization indicated
Pneumothorax.Definition: A disorder characterized by abnormal presence of air in the pleural cavity resulting in the collapse of the lung.

Postnasal drip.Definition: A disorder characterized by excessive mucous secretion in the back of the nasal cavity or throat, causing sore throat and/or coughing.

Productive cough.Grade 1: Occasional/minimal production of sputum with cough
Productive cough.Grade 2: Moderate sputum production; limiting instrumental ADL
Productive cough.Grade 3: Persistent or copious production of sputum; limiting self care ADL
Productive cough.Definition: A disorder characterized by expectorated secretions upon coughing.

Pulmonary edema.Grade 1: Radiologic findings only; minimal dyspnea on exertion
Pulmonary edema.Grade 2: Moderate dyspnea on exertion; medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Pulmonary edema.Grade 3: Severe dyspnea or dyspnea at rest; oxygen indicated; limiting self care ADL
Pulmonary edema.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory compromise; urgent intervention or intubation with ventilatory support indicated
Pulmonary edema.Definition: A disorder characterized by accumulation of fluid in the lung tissues that causes a disturbance of the gas exchange that may lead to respiratory failure.

Pulmonary fibrosis.Grade 1: Radiologic pulmonary fibrosis <25% of lung volume associated with hypoxia
Pulmonary fibrosis.Grade 2: Evidence of pulmonary hypertension; radiographic pulmonary fibrosis 25 - 50% associated with hypoxia
Pulmonary fibrosis.Grade 3: Severe hypoxia; evidence of right-sided heart failure; radiographic pulmonary fibrosis >50 - 75%
Pulmonary fibrosis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences (e.g., hemodynamic/pulmonary complications); intubation with ventilatory support indicated; radiographic pulmonary fibrosis >75% with severe honeycombing
