Pulmonary fibrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by the replacement of the lung tissue by connective tissue, leading to progressive dyspnea, respiratory failure or right heart failure.

Pulmonary fistula.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal communication between the lung and another organ or anatomic site.

Pulmonary hypertension.Grade 1: Minimal dyspnea; findings on physical exam or other evaluation
Pulmonary hypertension.Grade 2: Moderate dyspnea, cough; requiring evaluation by cardiac catheterization and medical intervention
Pulmonary hypertension.Grade 3: Severe symptoms, associated with hypoxia, right heart failure; oxygen indicated
Pulmonary hypertension.Grade 4: Life-threatening airway consequences; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., tracheotomy or intubation)
Pulmonary hypertension.Definition: A disorder characterized by an increase in pressure within the pulmonary circulation due to lung or heart disorder.

Respiratory failure.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent intervention, intubation, or ventilatory support indicated
Respiratory failure.Definition: A disorder characterized by impaired gas exchange by the respiratory system resulting in hypoxia and a decrease in oxygenation of the tissues that may be associated with an increase in arterial levels of carbon dioxide.

Respiratory, thoracic and mediastinal di.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Respiratory, thoracic and mediastinal di.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Respiratory, thoracic and mediastinal di.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Retinoic acid syndrome.Grade 1: Fluid retention; <3 kg of weight gain; intervention with fluid restriction and/or diuretics indicated
Retinoic acid syndrome.Grade 2: Moderate signs or symptoms; steroids indicated
Retinoic acid syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by weight gain, dyspnea, pleural and pericardial effusions, leukocytosis and/or renal failure originally described in patients treated with all-trans retinoic acid.

Rhinorrhea.Definition: A disorder characterized by excessive mucous secretions draining from the nose.

Sinus disorder.Grade 2: Symptomatic stenosis or edema/narrowing interfering with airflow; limiting instrumental ADL
Sinus disorder.Grade 3: Stenosis with significant nasal obstruction; limiting self care ADL
Sinus disorder.Grade 4: Necrosis of soft tissue or bone; urgent operative intervention indicated
Sinus disorder.Definition: A disorder characterized by involvement of the paranasal sinuses.

Sinus pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Sinus pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the face, between the eyes, or upper teeth originating from the sinuses.

Sleep apnea.Grade 1: Snoring and nocturnal sleep arousal without apneic periods
Sleep apnea.Grade 2: Moderate apnea and oxygen desaturation; excessive daytime sleepiness; medical evaluation indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Sleep apnea.Grade 3: Oxygen desaturation; associated with pulmonary hypertension; medical intervention indicated; limiting self care ADL
Sleep apnea.Grade 4: Cardiovascular or neuropsychiatric symptoms; urgent operative intervention indicated
Sleep apnea.Definition: A disorder characterized by cessation of breathing for short periods during sleep.

Sneezing.Definition: A disorder characterized by the involuntary expulsion of air from the nose.

Sore throat.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL; limiting ability to swallow
Sore throat.Definition: A disorder characterized by marked discomfort in the throat

Stridor.Grade 3: Respiratory distress limiting self care ADL; medical intervention indicated
Stridor.Grade 4: Life-threatening airway compromise; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., tracheotomy or intubation)
Stridor.Definition: A disorder characterized by a high pitched breathing sound due to laryngeal or upper airway obstruction.

Tracheal fistula.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal communication between the trachea and another organ or anatomic site.

Tracheal mucositis.Grade 1: Endoscopic findings only; minimal hemoptysis, pain, or respiratory symptoms
Tracheal mucositis.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Tracheal mucositis.Grade 3: Severe pain; hemorrhage or respiratory symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Tracheal mucositis.Definition: A disorder characterized by an inflammation or ulceration involving the mucous membrane of the trachea.

Tracheal stenosis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Tracheal stenosis.Grade 2: Symptomatic (e.g., noisy airway breathing), no respiratory distress; medical intervention indicated (e.g., steroids); limiting instrumental ADL
Tracheal stenosis.Grade 3: Stridor or respiratory distress limiting self care ADL; invasive intervention indicated (e.g., stent, laser)
Tracheal stenosis.Grade 4: Life-threatening airway compromise; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., tracheotomy or intubation)
Tracheal stenosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a narrowing of the trachea.

Voice alteration.Grade 1: Mild or intermittent change from normal voice
Voice alteration.Grade 2: Moderate or persistent change from normal voice; still understandable
Voice alteration.Grade 3: Severe voice changes including predominantly whispered speech; may require frequent repetition or face-to-face contact for understandability; may require assistive technology
Voice alteration.Definition: A disorder characterized by a change in the sound and/or speed of the voice.

Wheezing.Grade 1: Detectable airway noise with minimal symptoms
Wheezing.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms; medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Wheezing.Grade 3: Severe respiratory symptoms limiting self care ADL; oxygen therapy or hospitalization indicated
Wheezing.Definition: A disorder characterized by a high-pitched, whistling sound during breathing. It results from the narrowing or obstruction of the respiratory airways.

Alopecia.Grade 1: Hair loss of <50% of normal for that individual that is not obvious from a distance but only on close inspection; a different hair style may be required to cover the hair loss but it does not require a wig or hair piece to camouflage
Alopecia.Grade 2: Hair loss of >=50% normal for that individual that is readily apparent to others; a wig or hair piece is necessary if the patient desires to completely camouflage the hair loss; associated with psychosocial impact
Alopecia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in density of hair compared to normal for a given individual at a given age and body location.

Body odor.Grade 1: Mild odor; physician intervention not indicated; self care interventions
Body odor.Grade 2: Pronounced odor; psychosocial impact; patient seeks medical intervention
Body odor.Definition: A disorder characterized by an abnormal body smell resulting from the growth of bacteria on the body.

Bullous dermatitis.Grade 2: Blisters covering 10 - 30% BSA; painful blisters; limiting instrumental ADL
Bullous dermatitis.Grade 3: Blisters covering >30% BSA; limiting self care ADL
Bullous dermatitis.Grade 4: Blisters covering >30% BSA; associated with fluid or electrolyte abnormalities; ICU care or burn unit indicated
Bullous dermatitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the skin characterized by the presence of bullae which are filled with fluid.
Bullous dermatitis.Navigational Note: If infectious, consider Infections and infestations: Rash pustular or other site-specific Infections and infestations term.

Dry skin.Grade 1: Covering <10% BSA and no associated erythema or pruritus
Dry skin.Grade 2: Covering 10 - 30% BSA and associated with erythema or pruritus; limiting instrumental ADL
Dry skin.Grade 3: Covering >30% BSA and associated with pruritus; limiting self care ADL
Dry skin.Definition: A disorder characterized by flaky and dull skin; the pores are generally fine, the texture is a papery thin texture.

Eczema.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; additional medical intervention over baseline not indicated
Eczema.Grade 2: Moderate; topical or oral intervention indicated; additional medical intervention over baseline indicated
Eczema.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; IV intervention indicated
Eczema.Definition: A disorder characterized by skin which becomes itchy, red, inflamed, crusty, thick, scaly, and/or forms blisters.

Erythema multiforme.Grade 1: Target lesions covering <10% BSA and not associated with skin tenderness
Erythema multiforme.Grade 2: Target lesions covering 10 - 30% BSA and associated with skin tenderness
Erythema multiforme.Grade 3: Target lesions covering >30% BSA and associated with oral or genital erosions
Erythema multiforme.Grade 4: Target lesions covering >30% BSA; associated with fluid or electrolyte abnormalities; ICU care or burn unit indicated
Erythema multiforme.Definition: A disorder characterized by target lesions (a pink-red ring around a pale center).

Erythroderma.Grade 2: Erythema covering >90% BSA without associated symptoms; limiting instrumental ADL
Erythroderma.Grade 3: Erythema covering >90% BSA with associated symptoms (e.g., pruritus or tenderness); limiting self care ADL
Erythroderma.Grade 4: Erythema covering >90% BSA with associated fluid or electrolyte abnormalities; ICU care or burn unit indicated
Erythroderma.Definition: A disorder characterized by generalized inflammatory erythema and exfoliation. The inflammatory process involves > 90% of the body surface area.

Fat atrophy.Grade 2: Covering 10 - 30% BSA and associated with erythema or tenderness; limiting instrumental ADL
Fat atrophy.Grade 3: Covering >30% BSA; associated with erythema or tenderness; limiting self-care ADL
Fat atrophy.Definition: A disorder characterized by shrinking of adipose tissue.

Hair color changes.Definition: A disorder characterized by change in hair color or loss of normal pigmentation.

Hair texture abnormal.Definition: A disorder characterized by a change in the way the hair feels.

Hirsutism.Grade 1: In women, increase in length, thickness or density of hair in a male distribution that the patient is able to camouflage by periodic shaving, bleaching, or removal of hair
Hirsutism.Grade 2: In women, increase in length, thickness or density of hair in a male distribution that requires daily shaving or consistent destructive means of hair removal to camouflage; associated with psychosocial impact
Hirsutism.Definition: A disorder characterized by the presence of excess hair growth in women in anatomic sites where growth is considered to be a secondary male characteristic and under androgen control (beard, moustache, chest, abdomen).

Hyperhidrosis.Grade 1: Limited to one site (palms, soles, or axillae); self care interventions
Hyperhidrosis.Grade 2: Involving >1 site; patient seeks medical intervention; associated with psychosocial impact
Hyperhidrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by excessive sweating.
Hyperhidrosis.CTCAE v5.0 Change: Addition: Navigational note; Clarification: Grade 3, Definition

Hyperkeratosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a thickening of the outer layer of the skin.

Hypertrichosis.Grade 1: Increase in length, thickness or density of hair that the patient is either able to camouflage by periodic shaving or removal of hairs or is not concerned enough about the overgrowth to use any form of hair removal
Hypertrichosis.Grade 2: Increase in length, thickness or density of hair at least on the usual exposed areas of the body [face (not limited to beard/moustache area) plus/minus arms] that requires frequent shaving or use of destructive means of hair removal to camouflage; associated with psychosocial impact
Hypertrichosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by hair density or length beyond the accepted limits of normal in a particular body region, for a particular age or race.

Hypohidrosis.Grade 3: Increase in body temperature; limiting self care ADL
Hypohidrosis.Definition: A disorder characterized by reduced sweating.

Lipohypertrophy.Grade 2: Covering 10 - 30% BSA and associated tenderness; limiting instrumental ADL
Lipohypertrophy.Grade 3: Covering >30% BSA and associated tenderness and narcotics or NSAIDs indicated; lipohypertrophy; limiting self care ADL
Lipohypertrophy.Definition: A disorder characterized by hypertrophy of the subcutaneous adipose tissue at the site of multiple subcutaneous injections of insulin.

Nail changes.Definition: A disorder characterized by a change in the nails.

Nail discoloration.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only
Nail discoloration.Definition: A disorder characterized by a change in the color of the nail plate.

Nail loss.Grade 1: Asymptomatic separation of the nail bed from the nail plate or nail loss
Nail loss.Grade 2: Symptomatic separation of the nail bed from the nail plate or nail loss; limiting instrumental ADL
Nail loss.Definition: A disorder characterized by loss of all or a portion of the nail.

Nail ridging.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Nail ridging.Definition: A disorder characterized by vertical or horizontal ridges on the nails.

Pain of skin.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Pain of skin.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the skin.

Palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia syndro.Grade 1: Minimal skin changes or dermatitis (e.g., erythema, edema, or hyperkeratosis) without pain
Palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia syndro.Grade 2: Skin changes (e.g., peeling, blisters, bleeding, fissures, edema, or hyperkeratosis) with pain; limiting instrumental ADL
Palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia syndro.Grade 3: Severe skin changes (e.g., peeling, blisters, bleeding, fissures, edema, or hyperkeratosis) with pain; limiting self care ADL
Palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia syndro.Definition: A disorder characterized by redness, marked discomfort, swelling, and tingling in the palms of the hands or the soles of the feet. Also known as Hand-Foot Syndrome.

Photosensitivity.Grade 1: Painless erythema and erythema covering <10% BSA
Photosensitivity.Grade 3: Erythema covering >30% BSA and erythema with blistering; photosensitivity; oral corticosteroid therapy indicated; pain control indicated (e.g., narcotics or NSAIDs)
Photosensitivity.Definition: A disorder characterized by an increase in sensitivity of the skin to light.

Pruritus.Grade 1: Mild or localized; topical intervention indicated
Pruritus.Grade 2: Widespread and intermittent; skin changes from scratching (e.g., edema, papulation, excoriations, lichenification, oozing/crusts); oral intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Pruritus.Grade 3: Widespread and constant; limiting self care ADL or sleep; systemic corticosteroid or immunosuppressive therapy indicated
Pruritus.Definition: A disorder characterized by an intense itching sensation.

Purpura.Grade 1: Combined area of lesions covering <10% BSA
Purpura.Grade 2: Combined area of lesions covering 10 - 30% BSA; bleeding with trauma
Purpura.Grade 3: Combined area of lesions covering >30% BSA; spontaneous bleeding
Purpura.Definition: A disorder characterized by hemorrhagic areas of the skin and mucous membrane. Newer lesions appear reddish in color. Older lesions are usually a darker purple color and eventually become a brownish-yellow color.

Rash acneiform.Grade 1: Papules and/or pustules covering <10% BSA, which may or may not be associated with symptoms of pruritus or tenderness
Rash acneiform.Grade 2: Papules and/or pustules covering 10 - 30% BSA, which may or may not be associated with symptoms of pruritus or tenderness; associated with psychosocial impact; limiting instrumental ADL; papules and/or pustules covering > 30% BSA with or without mild symptoms
Rash acneiform.Grade 3: Papules and/or pustules covering >30% BSA with moderate or severe symptoms; limiting self-care ADL; associated with local superinfection with oral antibiotics indicated
Rash acneiform.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; papules and/or pustules covering any % BSA, which may or may not be associated with symptoms of pruritus or tenderness and are associated with extensive superinfection with IV antibiotics indicated
Rash acneiform.Definition: A disorder characterized by an eruption of papules and pustules, typically appearing in face, scalp, upper chest and back.

Rash maculo-papular.Grade 1: Macules/papules covering <10% BSA with or without symptoms (e.g., pruritus, burning, tightness)
Rash maculo-papular.Grade 2: Macules/papules covering 10 - 30% BSA with or without symptoms (e.g., pruritus, burning, tightness); limiting instrumental ADL; rash covering > 30% BSA with or without mild symptoms
Rash maculo-papular.Grade 3: Macules/papules covering >30% BSA with moderate or severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Rash maculo-papular.Definition: A disorder characterized by the presence of macules (flat) and papules (elevated). Also known as morbillform rash, it is one of the most common cutaneous adverse events, frequently affecting the upper trunk, spreading centripetally and associated with pruritis.

Scalp pain.Grade 3: Severe pain; limiting self care ADL
Scalp pain.Definition: A disorder characterized by a sensation of marked discomfort in the skin covering the top and the back of the head.

Skin and subcutaneous tissue disorders -.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Skin and subcutaneous tissue disorders -.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Skin and subcutaneous tissue disorders -.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Skin atrophy.Grade 1: Covering <10% BSA; associated with telangiectasias or changes in skin color
Skin atrophy.Grade 2: Covering 10 - 30% BSA; associated with striae or adnexal structure loss
Skin atrophy.Definition: A disorder characterized by the degeneration and thinning of the epidermis and dermis.

Skin hyperpigmentation.Grade 1: Hyperpigmentation covering <10% BSA; no psychosocial impact
Skin hyperpigmentation.Grade 2: Hyperpigmentation covering >10% BSA; associated psychosocial impact
Skin hyperpigmentation.Definition: A disorder characterized by darkening of the skin due to excessive melanin deposition.

Skin hypopigmentation.Grade 1: Hypopigmentation or depigmentation covering <10% BSA; no psychosocial impact
Skin hypopigmentation.Grade 2: Hypopigmentation or depigmentation covering >10% BSA; associated psychosocial impact
Skin hypopigmentation.Definition: A disorder characterized by loss of skin pigment (e.g., vitiligo).

Skin induration.Grade 1: Mild induration, able to move skin parallel to plane (sliding) and perpendicular to skin (pinching up)
Skin induration.Grade 2: Moderate induration, able to slide skin, unable to pinch skin; limiting instrumental ADL
Skin induration.Grade 3: Severe induration; unable to slide or pinch skin; limiting joint or orifice movement (e.g., mouth, anus); limiting self care ADL
Skin induration.Grade 4: Generalized; associated with signs or symptoms of impaired breathing or feeding
Skin induration.Definition: A disorder characterized by an area of hardness in the skin.

Skin ulceration.Grade 1: Combined area of ulcers <1 cm; nonblanchable erythema of intact skin with associated warmth or edema
Skin ulceration.Grade 2: Combined area of ulcers 1 - 2 cm; partial thickness skin loss involving skin or subcutaneous fat
Skin ulceration.Grade 3: Combined area of ulcers >2 cm; full-thickness skin loss involving damage to or necrosis of subcutaneous tissue that may extend down to fascia
Skin ulceration.Grade 4: Any size ulcer with extensive destruction, tissue necrosis, or damage to muscle, bone, or supporting structures with or without full thickness skin loss
Skin ulceration.Definition: A disorder characterized by a circumscribed, erosive lesion on the skin.

Stevens-Johnson syndrome.Grade 3: Skin sloughing covering <10% BSA with associated signs (e.g., erythema, purpura, epidermal detachment, and mucous membrane detachment)
Stevens-Johnson syndrome.Grade 4: Skin sloughing covering 10 - 30% BSA with associated signs (e.g., erythema, purpura, epidermal detachment and mucous membrane detachment)
Stevens-Johnson syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by less than 10% total body skin area separation of dermis. The syndrome is thought to be a hypersensitivity complex affecting the skin and the mucous membranes.

Subcutaneous emphysema.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Subcutaneous emphysema.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated
Subcutaneous emphysema.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL
Subcutaneous emphysema.Definition: A disorder characterized by air in the subcutaneous tissue.

Telangiectasia.Grade 2: Telangiectasias covering >=10% BSA; associated with psychosocial impact
Telangiectasia.Definition: A disorder characterized by local dilatation of small vessels resulting in red discoloration of the skin or mucous membranes.

Toxic epidermal necrolysis.Grade 4: Skin sloughing covering >=30% BSA with associated symptoms (e.g., erythema, purpura, or epidermal detachment)
Toxic epidermal necrolysis.Definition: A disorder characterized by greater than 30% total body skin area separation of dermis. The syndrome is thought to be a hypersensitivity complex affecting the skin and the mucous membranes.

Urticaria.Grade 1: Urticarial lesions covering <10% BSA; topical intervention indicated
Urticaria.Grade 2: Urticarial lesions covering 10 - 30% BSA; oral intervention indicated
Urticaria.Grade 3: Urticarial lesions covering >30% BSA; IV intervention indicated
Urticaria.Definition: A disorder characterized by an itchy skin eruption characterized by wheals with pale interiors and well-defined red margins.

Social circumstances - Other, specify.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Social circumstances - Other, specify.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Social circumstances - Other, specify.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Surgical and medical procedures - Other,.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Surgical and medical procedures - Other,.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Surgical and medical procedures - Other,.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Arterial thromboembolism.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; hemodynamic or neurologic instability; organ damage; loss of extremity(ies)
Arterial thromboembolism.Definition: A disorder characterized by occlusion of an arterial vessel by a blood clot that develops in an artery.
Arterial thromboembolism.Navigational Note: Consider Nervous system disorders: TIA or Stroke for CNS-related events or Cardiac disorders: Myocardial infarction

Capillary leak syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by leakage of intravascular fluids into the extravascular space. This syndrome is observed in patients who demonstrate a state of generalized leaky capillaries following shock syndromes, low-flow states, ischemia-reperfusion injuries, toxemias, medications, or poisoning. It can lead to generalized edema and multiple organ failure.

Flushing.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only
Flushing.Grade 3: Symptomatic, associated with hypotension and/or tachycardia; limiting self care ADL
Flushing.Definition: A disorder characterized by episodic reddening of the skin, especially face, neck, or chest.

Hematoma.Grade 2: Minimally invasive evacuation or aspiration indicated
Hematoma.Definition: A disorder characterized by a localized collection of blood, usually clotted, in an organ, space, or tissue, due to a break in the wall of a blood vessel.

Hot flashes.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Hot flashes.Definition: A disorder characterized by an uncomfortable and temporary sensation of intense body warmth, flushing, sometimes accompanied by sweating upon cooling.

Hypertension.Grade 3:
Adult: Systolic BP >=160 mm Hg or diastolic BP >=100 mm Hg; medical intervention indicated; more than one drug or more intensive therapy than previously used indicated;

Pediatric and adolescent: Systolic and/or diastolic > 5 mmHg above the 99th percentile
Hypertension.Grade 4: Adult and Pediatric: Life-threatening consequences (e.g., malignant hypertension, transient or permanent neurologic deficit, hypertensive crisis); urgent intervention indicated
Hypertension.Definition: A disorder characterized by a pathological increase in blood pressure.

Hypotension.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences and urgent intervention indicated
Hypotension.Definition: A disorder characterized by a blood pressure that is below the normal expected for an individual in a given environment.

Lymph leakage.Definition: A disorder characterized by the loss of lymph fluid into the surrounding tissue or body cavity.

Lymphedema.Grade 2: Marked discoloration; leathery skin texture; papillary formation; limiting instrumental ADL
Lymphedema.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL
Lymphedema.Definition: A disorder characterized by excessive fluid collection in tissues that causes swelling.

Lymphocele.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Lymphocele.Definition: A disorder characterized by a cystic lesion containing lymph.

Peripheral ischemia.Grade 2: Brief (<24 hrs) episode of ischemia managed medically and without permanent deficit
Peripheral ischemia.Grade 3: Prolonged (>=24 hrs) or recurring symptoms and/or invasive intervention indicated
Peripheral ischemia.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; evidence of end organ damage; urgent operative intervention indicated
Peripheral ischemia.Definition: A disorder characterized by impaired circulation to an extremity.

Phlebitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the wall of a vein.

Superficial thrombophlebitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by a blood clot and inflammation involving a superficial vein of the extremities.

Superior vena cava syndrome.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; incidental finding of SVC thrombosis
Superior vena cava syndrome.Grade 2: Symptomatic; medical intervention indicated (e.g., anticoagulation, radiation or chemotherapy)
Superior vena cava syndrome.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; multi-modality intervention indicated (e.g., anticoagulation, chemotherapy, radiation, stenting)
Superior vena cava syndrome.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; urgent multi-modality intervention indicated (e.g., lysis, thrombectomy, surgery)
Superior vena cava syndrome.Definition: A disorder characterized by obstruction of the blood flow in the superior vena cava. Signs and symptoms include swelling and cyanosis of the face, neck, and upper arms, cough, orthopnea and headache.

Thromboembolic event.Grade 1: Medical intervention not indicated (e.g., superficial thrombosis)
Thromboembolic event.Grade 3: Urgent medical intervention indicated (e.g., pulmonary embolism or intracardiac thrombus)
Thromboembolic event.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences with hemodynamic or neurologic instability
Thromboembolic event.Definition: A disorder characterized by occlusion of a vessel by a thrombus that has migrated from a distal site via the blood stream.
Thromboembolic event.Navigational Note: Consider Nervous system disorders: TIA or Stroke for CNS-related events. Use Vascular disorders: Arterial thromboembolism for arterial thrombi.

Vascular disorders - Other, specify.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Vascular disorders - Other, specify.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated; limiting age-appropriate instrumental ADL
Vascular disorders - Other, specify.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated; limiting self care ADL

Vasculitis.Grade 3: Severe symptoms, medical intervention indicated (e.g., steroids)
Vasculitis.Grade 4: Life-threatening consequences; evidence of peripheral or visceral ischemia; urgent intervention indicated
Vasculitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation involving the wall of a vessel.
